original.question: What's the best aspect to use when I'm fighting enemies up close as a hunter?
original.reference: According to the article, Aspect of the Monkey was the best Animal Aspect to use while engaged in melee combat prior to attaining Aspect of the Dragonhawk. It was a defensive aspect that increased dodge and lowered your damage taken when fighting enemies up close.
snippets: Aspect of the Monkey: Aspect of the Monkey was a defensive Animal Aspect for increasing dodge; also the first aspect a hunter used to receive from training. Uses This was the best Animal Aspect to use while engaged in melee combat prior to attaining Aspect of the Dragonhawk. Lowered your damage taken. Limitations Shares a 1-second cooldown with other hunter aspects. Warriors can potentially do more damage to you with the aspect on due to Overpower. Upgrades Improved Aspect of the Monkey (Exotic Beasts Talent): Increases the Dodge bonus of your Aspect of the Monkey by 2% per rank (3 ranks total). Patch changes See also Aspect of the Monkey (Fires of Outland), TCG card References External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Aspect of the Chameleon: Aspect of the Chameleon is a hunter ability, learned with the item The Art of Concealment. It is a disruptive PvP ability, removing the hunter from tracking hunter (Track Humanoids) or druid's (Track Humanoid) minimap for 1 minute. Uses This aspect can be used in Warsong Gulch when assaulting; defending hunters and druids would often turn on their tracking so they locate and ping runners or target enemies before enemies appear on screen. Patch changes External links Category:Hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Aspect of the Pack: Aspect of the Pack increases the party and raid's movement speed but makes them vulnerable to daze; a group version of Aspect of the Cheetah. Glyphs Notes This ability allows players to get to places faster and move quicker in battle. A handy speed boost indoors (where mounts do not work) especially after instance wipes. Generally, a hunter should wait until the last member of the group enters an area before activating this Aspect; the stragglers will eventually catch up to those in front, who will then get the buff themselves. By level 20, players have access to mounts which let them travel at +60% speed (though they are not usable in combat while this Aspect is). Your party member(s) will be dazed when hit, which is very annoying and potentially lethal (hence the term "Aspect of the Dazed" or "Aspect of the Wipe"). A hunter must be careful when to toggle off this ability as to not slow party members. There are addons that can automatically remove the aspect when someone is dazed. Aspect of the Pack does not increase speed if swimming, nor does it affect mount speed. Patch changes External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells Category:Auras Category:Snares and slows

Aspect of the Fox: Aspect of the Fox was an Animal Aspect that allowed all members of the hunter's party or raid to use any casted ability while moving. Notes If timed properly, a hunter could use this to mount up while moving. Patch changes External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Animal Aspects: An Animal Aspect is a self-buff that is modeled after different types of animals. The ability to assume the aspect of an animal is exclusive to hunters. Hunters can learn a total of three or four different Aspects, depending on specialization. Varieties Aspect of the Chameleon: The Hunter takes on the aspect of a chameleon, becoming untrackable. Aspect of the Cheetah: Increases the hunter's movement speed by 90% for 3 sec, and then by 30% for another 9 sec. Aspect of the Eagle (Survival): Grants the hunter and their pet 10% increased Critical Strike chance on all abilities, and increases their chance to gain charges of Mongoose Bite by 100%. Aspect of the Turtle: Deflects all attacks and reduces all damage the hunter take by 30% for 8 sec, but they cannot attack. Aspect of the Wild (Beast Mastery): Grants the hunter and their pet 10 Focus per 1 sec and 10% increased critical strike chance on all attacks for 10 sec. Past varieties This aspect was removed in Patch 7.0.3: Aspect of the Pack: The hunter's group (including the hunter) takes on the characteristics of a pack of cheetahs, increasing all their movement speeds by 30%. If a pack member takes damage, that member will be dazed for 4 sec. This aspect was removed in Patch 6.2.0: Aspect of the Fox: The hunter takes on the aspects of a fox, allowing your party and raid members to cast while moving for 6 seconds. Redesigned and re-added in Patch 6.0.2.
original.response:
**Aspect of the Turtle** is your best choice for close-quarters combat. It deflects all attacks and reduces all damage you take by 30% for 8 seconds, making it the ultimate defensive tool when enemies are right on top of you. The tradeoff is that you can't attack while it's active, but that defensive wall is invaluable when things get hairy.

If you're looking for something you can use while still dealing damage, **Aspect of the Monkey** was historically the go-to for melee situations before better options became available. It increased your dodge chance to reduce damage taken, letting you stay offensive while still getting defensive benefits.

The choice depends on whether you need that hard defensive reset (Turtle) or prefer to keep fighting while getting modest damage reduction (Monkey).

The Eagle's Essence: The Eagle's Essence is part of the Druid's quest chain. Objectives Defeat the Guardian of the Eagle and obtain the Essence of the Eagle. Use Arthorn's Sparrowhawk Whistle to call Arthorn's Sparrowhawk to relay news of your success. If you lose Arthorn's Sparrowhawk Whistle, return to Arthorn Windsong at the Evergrove for a replacement. Essence of the Eagle Arthorn's Sparrowhawk Whistle (provided) Description thumb|The Shrine of the Eagle The Book of the Raven tells us the raven's followers captured and bound the spirits of his rivals within stone shrines. A guardian was set to watch over each, lest the raven's enemies attempt to free them. The first of the shrines is the eagle's, found in the hills to the south of Lake Jorune and west of Stonebreaker Hold in Terokkar Forest. The tablet says that the eagle fought valiantly, but was overcome by a swarm of enemies. The armor and endurance of your bear form is well-suited to this fight. Rewards You will receive: +150 reputation with Cenarion Expedition Progress Did you succeed in freeing the essence of the eagle spirit? original.response:
# The Eagle's Essence Quest Guide

The **Shrine of the Eagle** is located in Terokkar Forest at coordinates **46, 45** — in the hills south of Lake Jorune and west of Stonebreaker Hold. Once you get there, simply **click on the shrine to summon the Guardian of the Eagle**.

The fight itself is tough but definitely soloable. The quest description suggests using your **bear form** for the extra armor and endurance, as the tablet indicates the eagle was overcome by swarms of enemies. You'll need to defeat the Guardian and collect the **Essence of the Eagle** it drops, then use **Arthorn's Sparrowhawk Whistle** (which you're given for the quest) to call Arthorn's Sparrowhawk and relay your success back to Arthorn Windsong at Evergrove. If you lose the whistle, you can grab a replacement from Arthorn. You'll get +150 Cenarion Expedition reputation for completing this quest.

Yes, absolutely! **Gloves of the Vanquished Defender** is a Tier 5 armor token, and you can trade it in for actual gear pieces.

You have two main options. You can exchange it with **Kalara at Shattrath's Aldor Rise** or **Veynna Dawnstar on the Scryer's Tier** for Tier 5 PvE armor pieces. Alternatively, if you're interested in PvP gear, you can take it to **Olus at the Isle of Quel'Danas** to exchange it for Season 2 PvP items instead.

So you're not stuck with the token—it's designed as a currency to get you the actual gear you want. Just head to one of those NPCs depending on whether you want PvE or PvP equipment!

original.question: How do I turn my Lightwell into Lightspring as a priest?
original.reference: To turn your Lightwell into Lightspring as a priest, you need to use the Glyph of Lightspring. This glyph transforms Lightwell into Lightspring.
snippets: Lightspring: Lightspring is a priest ability granted by Glyph of Lightspring. It transforms Lightwell into Lightspring. Patch changes External links

Lightwell: thumb|Lightwell Lightwell is a Holy priest ability that conjures a very shiny healing well. Every second it will attempt to give party and raid members a 6 second buff (Lightwell Renew) that heals for a notable amount while the buff is active. It is an exclusive choice node with Lightweaver. History thumb|In the TCG. Prior to patch 5.4.0, Lightwell did not heal targets automatically but instead required players to click on the well in order to receive healing. This functionality engendered a significant amount of complaints from priests and non-priests alike, although some argued that the greatest factor in the failure of Lightwell as a useful healing tool lay in the lack of education of the playerbase. Over time Blizzard granted progressive buffs to Lightwell, but the spell failed to receive whole-hearted acceptance by most players due to the requirement for their own active interaction, preferring to leave the healing to the healers. Patch 5.0.4 introduced Glyph of Lightspring, providing priests with an alternative functionality for the well. Apparently this alternative was well-received, as with Patch 5.4.0 the new design was adopted as the default functionality of Lightwell. Trivia One of the loading screen tips once read "Click on the Lightwell... unless it's a Lightspring!". The tip was originally simply "Click on the Lightwell!", but this was changed some time after the introduction of the now defunct Glyph of Lightspring.
Glyph of Lightspring: Glyph of Lightspring is created with Inscription (260). Patch changes External links Category:Removed priest glyphs
Glyph of Lightwell: Source This item is created with Inscription (150); discovered through Research: Jadefire Ink Patch changes External links Category:Removed priest glyphs

Argent Lightwell Charm: thumb|Argent Lightwell Charm The Argent Lightwell Charm is a :lightwell provided as an objective for The Corpsebeasts. Abilities Patch changes External links Argent Lightwell Charm Argent Lightwell Category:Lightwell
original.response:
# Lightspring and Lightwell

Unfortunately, you can't do this anymore—**Glyph of Lightspring has been removed from the game**. This glyph used to transform your Lightwell into Lightspring, which automatically healed nearby party and raid members instead of requiring them to click on the well.

The glyph was so popular that Blizzard eventually made its functionality the default behavior of Lightwell in patch 5.4.0. Before that change, Lightwell was notoriously unpopular because players had to manually click it to receive healing, which most people refused to do. Once the automatic healing was implemented as standard, the glyph became obsolete and was removed from the game.

So the good news: **modern Lightwell already works like the old Lightspring did**—it automatically grants nearby allies a healing buff every second without requiring any clicking.

original.response:
# Getting Curious Coins for Xur'ios

You can earn **Curious Coins** through quite a few different activities. The main sources are **completing 4 World Quests for Emissary caches**, **winning battlegrounds**, and **defeating bosses in raids, dungeons, and world bosses**. You'll also get them from **Mythic+ Challenger's Caches** if you're into higher-end dungeons.

Beyond combat, there are some more passive options too. **Rare spawns and treasure chests** scattered throughout the Broken Isles and Argus will drop coins, and if you're working on faction reputation, you'll earn them from **Paragon caches when you hit every 10,000 reputation milestone** with a faction (post-patch). So depending on what content you prefer, there's usually a way to farm coins that fits your playstyle.

original.response:
# Desolation Battlegear Farm Locations

**Desolation Battlegear** is a mail Dungeon Set 3 spread across several Burning Crusade-era dungeons. Here's where to farm each piece:

**Greaves of Desolation** drop from **Talon King Ikiss** in **Sethekk Halls**. The **Hauberk of Desolation** (chest piece) comes from **Epoch Hunter** in the **Old Hillsbrad Foothills** wing of the **Caverns of Time** on Heroic difficulty. **Pauldrons of Desolation** are looted from **Quagmirran** in **Slave Pens** on Heroic mode, and the **Gauntlets of Desolation** drop from **Warchief Kargath Bladefist** in **The Shattered Halls**. Finally, you can get another piece from **Aeonus** in **The Black Morass**.

This set is one of the few Dungeon Set 3 pieces that isn't class-specific, making it flexible for mail-wearing classes like Hunters and Shamans. You'll need to run these dungeons on Heroic difficulty for most pieces, so make sure you're geared appropriately before farming.

original.question: Where can I buy ram mounts as an Alliance player, and what do I need to get them?
original.reference: Alliance players can buy ram mounts from Amberstill Ranch, east of Ironforge in northern Dun Morogh. To purchase Apprentice-level rams (60% speed increase), you need the Apprentice riding skill and, if you're not a Dwarf, Ironforge reputation. For Journeyman-level rams (100% speed increase), you need the Journeyman riding skill with the same reputation requirements for non-Dwarves.

original.response:
# Getting Ram Mounts as Alliance

**Most Alliance ram mounts come from Amberstill Ranch**, located east of Ironforge in northern Dun Morogh. You have two tiers to choose from:

The **Apprentice tier** includes the Brown Ram, Gray Ram, and White Ram, which give a 60% speed increase. These require Apprentice riding skill. If you're not a Dwarf, you'll also need reputation with Ironforge.

The **Journeyman tier** offers Swift Brown Ram, Swift Gray Ram, and Swift White Ram with a 100% speed increase. You'll need Journeyman riding skill and face the same reputation requirement if you're a non-Dwarf character.

Beyond the basic vendor mounts, you have other options: the **Argent Tournament** sells the Ironforge Ram and Swift Violet Ram if you've completed the Champion of Ironforge achievement (or if you're a Dwarf). **PvP players** can grab the Black War Ram from Stormwind for 15 Marks of Honor, and there's also the Stormpike Battle Charger from Alterac Valley for the same cost. If you want something special, the **Swift Brewfest Ram** is available to all players during the Brewfest event by defeating Coren Direbrew in Blackrock Depths.

original.question: I'm a new player and I keep hearing about Gadgetzan. What exactly is that place and why can both Horde and Alliance go there?
original.reference: Gadgetzan is the capital city of the Steamwheedle Cartel, the largest goblin cartel, located in the Tanaris desert. Both Horde and Alliance can go there because goblins prioritize profit above loyalty and consider Gadgetzan neutral territory in any Horde-Alliance conflict. Both governments officially recognize goblin neutrality, and the city's streets are heavily patrolled by goblin bruisers to enforce peace and protect their trade.
snippets: Gadgetzan: Although goblin neutrality is almost universally acknowledged, there are still those who seek to sow chaos and anarchy. For Gadgetzan, this came in the form of the Wastewander bandits, a gang of miscreants who occupied the Waterspring Field and the Noonshade Ruins on the northeast Tanaris. Few goblins care about ancient ruins (unless they have treasure) – for all they care, the bandits could have the old blocks of stone, however, the Waterspring Field was vital to Gadgetzan's survival in the desert which provided them with the liquid gold. Water towers out in the field were constructed and maintained by the Gadgetzan Water Company under the blazing heat of the desert sun, so the goblins weren't going to give up their hard-earned towers that easily. However, the goblin bruisers needed to stay in town to keep the gnomes' collective Napoleonic-complex from getting out of hand and to stop the seemingly endless dueling among the various visitors from disrupting business. Therefore, it fell to brave mercenaries from all corners of the world to help the goblins in their time of utmost need. By this time, the city was briefly visited by Kova Broadhorn, Miles Corebender, and Bellthazor.Miles to Go Cataclysm thumb|Gadgetzan from the air after the Cataclysm. thumb|The new Gadgetzan docks.

original.response:
**Gadgetzan is a goblin-controlled trading hub** located in the Tanaris desert, and it's one of the few truly neutral cities in Warcraft because goblins maintain strict neutrality in the conflict between the Horde and Alliance. Both factions can visit because the goblins prioritize profit over politics—they're happy to do business with anyone willing to pay.

**The city serves as a crucial desert outpost** with vital resources like water towers maintained by the Gadgetzan Water Company, making it an essential stop for travelers crossing the harsh Tanaris landscape. Beyond survival necessities, it's a major trading center where you can find quests, vendors, and NPCs from various backgrounds. The goblins keep order by maintaining strict rules against disruption—they even have to manage disputes between visiting adventurers and prevent the gnomes living there from causing trouble.

**What makes Gadgetzan interesting is that it's pragmatic rather than idealistic.** The goblins have defended it from threats like the Wastewander bandits not out of moral principle, but because those bandits threatened their water supply and business interests. This mercenary attitude is why both Horde and Alliance are welcome—as long as you're not actively causing problems in town, the goblins don't care which side you're on. It's one of the best examples of how goblin culture differs from the rest of Azeroth's factions.

original.question: Hey, I'm doing Terrace of Endless Spring for the first time. What's the deal with Lei Shi? Is she hard and what should our raid know before we pull her?
original.reference: Lei Shi is the third boss in Terrace of Endless Spring and involves challenging tank swap mechanics with strong adds that revive periodically. Your raid should position in a triangle formation with tanks on separate corners to minimize Spray damage, and be prepared for frequent tank switches when she casts Protect at 80%, 60%, 40%, and 20% health. The main challenge is managing the reviving Corrupted Protectors while handling the tank swap mechanics.
snippets: Lei Shi: Lei Shi is the third boss in Terrace of Endless Spring. She is a sha-corrupted water elemental who will help players with the final battle with the Sha of Fear after being freed from corruption. The fight involves challenging tank swap mechanics and strong adds that revive periodically. Once the sha is defeated, her image appears on the main platform. She spawns by defeating the Corrupted Protectors, she ripples nervously upon their defeat, after the player causes AOE damage it will make her ripple to the point it becomes fully visible. Background Adventure Guide For countless years, this friendly water spirit frolicked in the Fountains of Endless Spring with childlike wonder and glee. When fighting suddenly broke out in this peaceful place, Lei Shi saw the crystal-clear water turn cloudy and murky, and terror fell over her mind. Spells and abilities Notes Strategy Position the raid in a triangle, with one tank on one corner, the second tank on another corner and the remainder of the raid on the third corner (similar to the positioning for Phase 2 of Beth'tilac in the Firelands). The aim of this positioning is to have only the active tank affected by Spray. The two tanks will regularly need to taunt off each other to prevent Spray from stacking too high. When Lei Shi casts Protect (at 80%, 60%, 40% and 20%), perform a tank switch, and have the off-tank pick up the s (Lei Shi continues to cast Spray while the Protectors are up).

Terrace of Endless Spring: The Terrace of Endless Spring (or the Terrace of Eternal Spring)The Emperor's Gift is one of the three launch raids of World of Warcraft: Mists of Pandaria. It allows players to finally defeat the Sha of Fear, a "super super huge raid boss" who "has turned other local creatures into Sha",https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YtEsFzry5yc after driving it out of the Heart of Fear. The raid is relatively small with four boss encounters: the Protectors of the Endless, a trio of Jinyu; Tsulong, a cloud serpent; Lei Shi, a water spirit; and the Sha of Fear itself as the final boss. The raid is located at the midpoint of the Veiled Stair, a mountain pass between the northeastern corner of the Valley of the Four Winds and the southern part of the Kun-Lai Summit. The meeting stone and a flight path are located just down a short hill from the raid dungeon entrance, near the Tavern in the Mists deep within the Stair. Normal mode opened November 13, 2012. Raid Finder and Heroic modes opened on November 20, 2012. Raid Finder difficulty requires an average item level of 470.

original.response:
# Lei Shi - Third Boss of Terrace of Endless Spring

**Lei Shi is a sha-corrupted water elemental** and the third boss encounter in the raid. She's a pretty technical fight mechanically, so your raid definitely needs to know what's coming.

The key mechanic is **aggressive tank swapping**. Lei Shi casts a spell called Spray that stacks on the active tank, so your two tanks need to taunt back and forth regularly to keep the stacks manageable. Beyond that, she'll summon Protectors at 80%, 60%, 40%, and 20% health — when this happens, swap to the off-tank and have them pick up those adds while the main tank continues dealing with Lei Shi. The raid will need to handle consistent adds that revive periodically, so coordination is important.

**For positioning**, set up in a triangle formation with one tank in each corner and the rest of the raid in the third corner. This minimizes how many people get hit by her Spray ability. Make sure your tanks are communicating clearly about taunt swaps so Spray doesn't get out of hand.

Once you burn through her health and defeat the Sha of Fear afterward, **Lei Shi actually helps you** — she's freed from corruption and assists in that final encounter. So you're not just beating a corrupted spirit; you're actually saving her. Good luck with the fight!

original.question: How is Freezing Arrow different from just laying a regular Freezing Trap, and when would I want to use it instead?
original.reference: Freezing Arrow allows hunters to place a Freezing Trap at a distance using a targeting reticule, rather than having to be in melee range to lay a regular trap. This is particularly useful because it doesn't require the mob to be targeted or in focus, enabling hunters to crowd control ranged mobs, re-establish CC when a previous trap breaks, and chain-trap more flexibly without disengaging from their main target.
snippets: Freezing Arrow: Freezing Arrow was a Marksmanship hunter honor talent, available at rank 43. Notes This ability is essentially a ranged version of Freezing Trap for Marksmanship, with some key differences: the disorient effect also applies to nearby enemies, it doesn't have an arming delay, and can be used from a distance. Tips and tactics When in Arena, the Freezing Arrow is extremely useful, since casters who have to stand in one place will always fall prey to its icy grip. Even for classes who are mobile, you can still attempt to predict their path of travel and intercept them with a freezing arrow. This method is especially useful for paladins, who will likely blow both their Divine Shield and Honorable Medallion to escape its grip. Patch changes External links Category:Freezes

Freezing Arrow (hunter ability): Freezing Arrow is a hunter ability which can place a Freezing Trap at a distance. Notes This ability is essentially a marriage of the Flare and Freezing Trap abilities. Upon activating the ability, you are presented with a targeting reticule that shows the placement and radius of your trap. Once fired, it can take between one and two seconds to land, and an additional one second for the trap to arm. The trap acts exactly like a trap laid by Freezing Trap. This shares a cooldown with other trap abilities and Black Arrow. Improvements (See Freezing Trap for improvements.) Tips and tactics thumbnail|left|Troll Hunter firing a Freezing Arrow at a Void Summoner in Aldur'thar While the utility of this ability is limited against other players, it excels in group or solo questing, and is marginally useful in instances and raids. Some specific situations where it can prove useful are as follows. Against groups that have a ranged DPS mob, the hunter can place the trap to crowd control the ranged mob, which allows a pet or group member to aggro melee mobs without fear. When a mob breaks a previous trap or crowd control unexpectedly, the hunter may fire the Arrow as desired, to re-establish CC, without disengaging from the main target being killed. This is because, unlike many other crowd-control abilities, Freezing Arrow does not require having the mob as a target or focus. This also enables more flexible chain-trapping (see Freezing Trap for more details).

Freezing Trap: thumb|In the TCG Freezing Trap is a level 10 hunter ability. This trap offers long-duration crowd control that breaks on damage. Hunters who start in Exile's Reach unlock this by completing A Hunter's Trap. Notes and trivia Freezing Trap has a PvP duration of 8 seconds and is subject to diminishing returns. Immunities such as Ice Block and Divine Shield can break Freezing Trap's effect. Using Tame Beast on a frozen target will not break the effect. Freezing Trap is a common Hunter spell in Hearthstone. The flavor text reads: "'Dang, that's cold.' - appropriate response to Freezing Trap, or a mean joke." original.response:
**Freezing Arrow** is the ranged version of Freezing Trap that lets you place traps at a distance without needing to be in melee range. The key differences are:

**Tactical advantages:** You can cast it from afar with a targeting reticule showing the trap's placement and radius, it doesn't have an arming delay (it takes 1-2 seconds to land, then 1 second to arm), and the disorient effect actually hits nearby enemies too—not just the target that triggers it. In PvP arenas, this makes it devastating against stationary casters and lets you predict enemy movement to intercept them.

**When to use it:** Freezing Arrow shines in group content and questing where you need flexibility. Unlike regular Freezing Trap, you don't need to target or focus the mob to fire it, which means you can chain-trap more effectively and re-establish crowd control without disengaging from your main target. It's particularly useful against ranged DPS mobs in groups—you can freeze them while your pet or group handles melee enemies without spreading aggro. The main limitation is that it shares a cooldown with your other trap abilities and Black Arrow, so you're not getting bonus control—just more tactical placement options.

original.response:
# Zandalari Bladeguards

**Location and Variants**

Zandalari Bladeguards are the standard guards of the Zandalari Empire, primarily found in **Dazar'alor and Zuldazar**. During the Alliance War Campaign, you'll encounter them in specific locations like Warport Rastari and Xibala. There are also specialized variants—**Zeb'ahari Bladeguards** patrol Zuldazar, while **Wingrider Bladeguards** serve as mounted guards in the same region. You'll also find **Bladeguard Elites** throughout Zuldazar.

**Combat Abilities**

The standard Zandalari Bladeguard uses **Rending Cut** and **Mortal Strike**, a physical attack that reduces healing received by 25% for 10 seconds. They can also **Regenerate**, healing themselves every 2 seconds for 6 seconds. Specialized variants have their own techniques—Zeb'ahari Bladeguards can **Net** enemies to immobilize them for 5 seconds, while Wingrider Bladeguards use **Axe Flurry** to throw a series of axes at targets. Bladeguard Elites utilize the same **Rending Cut** as their standard counterparts.

original.question: What dropped from Onyxia?
original.reference: Onyxia is a black dragon who is the daughter of Deathwing. She dropped tier 2 headpieces for all classes, the Onyxia Scale Cloak, and had a chance to drop the Deathbringer sword and the Vis'kag the Bloodletter. She was also known for dropping a very rare mount called Onyxia's Lair.
snippets: Onyxia: Onyxia serves as one of the two secondary antagonists (alongside her brother Nefarian) in classic World of Warcraft and one of the supporting antagonists in Cataclysm. Biography Chronormu's visage thumb|Onyxia in Hearthstone. Onyxia believed that humans were the mortal race that posed the greatest threat to dragonkind. When deciding on her mortal guise, she chose the form of an attractive female human so that she could manipulate their kind and allow her to seize all she wanted from them. At some point, the bronze dragon Chronormu—who could not make up his mind on what mortal form to choose during his upcoming Visage Day ceremony—sought an audience with Onyxia in the caverns of the Obsidian Dragonshrine, where the daughter of Deathwing was overseeing drakonid laborers and sparring drakes. Onyxia scoffed at Chronormu's view that dragons chose a visage to better relate to the mortal races, stating that it sounded "like something Nozdormu would teach his whelps". She assumed her human form and continued that no visage could change a dragon's nature as a hunter and killer. Like their other abilities, shapeshifting was a tool to take what they wanted from the young races. Though Chronormu protested that this was not how he wanted mortals to see him, Onyxia offered to let him stay by her side and teach him everything he needed to know about mortals and allow him to become the greatest member of his flight, great enough that even Nozdormu would kneel before him.

Head of Onyxia: thumb|Onyxia's Head Mounted in Stormwind Head of Onyxia begins the quest A Most Puzzling Circumstance. Source This item drops from Onyxia in Onyxia's Lair. Only one head drops per kill. Notes As of Patch 3.2.2, turning in this quest no longer gives the city-wide buff, Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer, as it did in the original level 60 quest. Patch changes Category:Onyxia's Lair items Category:Heads

Onyxia (tactics): Onyxia is a level ?? black dragon boss in Onyxia's Lair. Abilities Phase One Onyxia melees for about 12,000-15,000 on plate. Onyxia is tauntable. Phase Two thumb|Deep Breath IS random! Onyxia takes off at 65%, has no threat table, cannot be attacked from melee, and uses the following abilities: Phase Three Onyxia lands at 40% HP. She regains all her phase one abilities and gains some additional abilities. Spawn Onyxian Whelps - Small numbers of whelps will continue to spawn in phase 3. Strategy frame|Map of Onyxia's lair, showing raid positioning for Phases 1 & 3This is (still) a very simple encounter overall. For strategy videos of both the 10-man and 25-man encounter, look below the loot table on this page. You ideally want 2 tanks, 2 healers, 3 melee DPS, and 3 ranged DPS for 10 man, and 2 tanks, 5 healers, 8 melee DPS, and 10 ranged DPS for 25 man. Phase 1 The tank should run slightly ahead of the rest of the raid and grab Onyxia, quickly turning her away from the raid and then backing off to the Northern wall, where the tank will position themselves with their back to the wall to minimise the effect of the knockback from Wing Buffet. Only the tank should be in front of Onyxia, to minimize damage from fire breath and cleaving. During phase one, the only damage to the raid should be upon the tank. Ranged DPS and healers need to stay to either side of her, fairly far back.

Spawn of Onyxia: __NOTOC__ Spawn of Onyxia are black whelps located in the Wyrmbog in Dustwallow Marsh. Pet Journal "These vicious fire-breathing whelps escaped Onyxia's Lair and fled into the surrounding region. Handle with extreme caution." Battles Patch changes External links Category:Black whelps Category:Critters Category:Dustwallow Marsh mobs

Onyxia's Lair: Onyxia's Lair is a level 80 raid dungeon located in the Wyrmbog, Dustwallow Marsh. It is home to Onyxia, the brood mother of the black dragonflight. Onyxia's Lair was originally a level 60, 40-player raid dungeon, one of two that were available upon release of World of Warcraft (the other being Molten Core). It was re-tuned as a level 80 raid dungeon in patch 3.2.2 in honor of World of Warcraft's 5-year anniversary. Like all other Wrath of the Lich King raid dungeons, it has both 10-man and 25-man modes. Lore Onyxia built the lair. The entrance to the lair was carved to look like the maw of the broodmother herself, with its inner pathway being magma-lined and the ground cracked, glowing with fire from beneath. The arching stone ceiling groans and shifts, creating constant rockfalls. The walls spill lava where the heat can't be contained.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 148 Onyxia is the daughter of the mighty Black Aspect, Deathwing, and sister of the scheming Nefarian, Lord of Blackrock Spire. Whereas her father favors brute force, and her brother creates twisted abominations to carry out his will, Onyxia prefers subtle manipulation, and delights in meddling in the political affairs of mortals, using her charm and power to influence such delicate matters between the different races to her own ends. Some believe that Onyxia has even assumed an alias once used by her father - the title of the royal House Prestor.
original.response:
**Onyxia's Head** was the notable quest item that dropped from her. Only one head dropped per kill, and it was used to begin the quest "A Most Puzzling Circumstance." In the original level 60 encounter, turning in this quest awarded players the city-wide buff **Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer**, though this was removed as of Patch 3.2.2 when the raid was re-tuned for level 80.

Beyond the quest item, Onyxia's Lair had standard raid loot drops like other boss encounters, though the specific gear rewards aren't detailed in the available information.

original.question: What is Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker?
original.reference: Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is a legendary one-handed sword from vanilla WoW. It required players to collect two bindings that dropped from Garr and Baron Geddon in Molten Core, then complete a quest chain involving Highlord Demitrian. The weapon is known for its powerful proc that reduces enemy threat generation and chains lightning to nearby enemies. It became a famous meme in the WoW community.
snippets: Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker: thumb|Thunderfury's owner, Wray. Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is the legendary sword once wielded by Prince Thunderaan, Prince of Air. Equipping the sword awards the Feat of Strength, Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (achievement). It is currently the only legendary weapon that is available to all classes. History Ragnaros the Firelord, and his lieutenants Garr and Baron Geddon, defeated Thunderaan, Prince of Air, after the First Age of Creation, during the Elemental Sundering. Ragnaros was unable to fully consume Thunderaan’s essence, so he forced what little remained into a talisman of elemental binding and shattered it. He gave the two halves to his lieutenants. Ages passed, and somehow the talisman halves found their way into the hands of Highlord Demitrian, a follower of Thunderaan. He recombined the talisman but was unable to release his master. Instead, he did the best he could: He crafted a blade to serve as a vessel for his master's essence. Nik the Mountain can be seen wielding the blade. When the Earthen Ring decides to resurrect Thunderaan to replace the fallen Al'Akir, a shaman adventurer takes Thunderfury from the corpse of its last wielder — a dwarf warrior called Wray who died while facing RagemawBlessed Blade of the Windseeker — in Azsuna and brings it to the Vortex Pinnacle.

Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (quest item): Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is formed through combining Damaged Thunderfury with the Heart of Skywall. Its power was used to bring Thunderaan back during the third invasion of the Burning Legion.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 107 Objective of Recharging the Blade Patch changes References External links

Inflatable Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker: thumb|Inflatable Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker Inflatable Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker was found inside Anniversary Gift (old) during WoW's 11th Anniversary. During WoW's 12th Anniversary, it could be purchased from in Stormwind City or in Orgrimmar at a cost of . When used, it equips the player who uses it with a replica of the infamous Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker. It also rewards the achievement Did Someone Say...? the first time it is used. I knew this day would come! At last, humanity understands the power of the Windseeker! When Prince Thunderaan was slain at the hands of treacherous mortals, his soul was trapped within the sacred blade Thunderfury. To resurrect my master you will need to find his sword and return it to Skywall. Last I heard, the betrayer who stole my master's sword traveled to the Broken Isles to fight demons. However, it has been damaged and the elemental soul within has diminished in power. Time may very well be running out... Source This item drops from Prince Thunderaan in The Crystal Vale in Silithus. As a quest objective Rise, Thunderfury! Patch changes External links
original.response:
# Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker

**Thunderfury is a legendary sword that once belonged to Prince Thunderaan, Prince of Air.** The blade itself serves as a vessel for Thunderaan's essence — when Ragnaros defeated and nearly consumed the prince during the Elemental Sundering, he couldn't fully destroy him. Instead, Ragnaros trapped what remained of Thunderaan in a talisman and shattered it. Ages later, a follower named Highlord Demitrian recombined the talisman halves and crafted the blade to contain his master's spirit.

**It's the only legendary weapon in World of Warcraft available to all classes**, making it uniquely accessible regardless of your character. Equipping it rewards the Feat of Strength achievement. The blade has been wielded by various warriors throughout history, including a dwarf named Wray who fell in the Broken Isles before a shaman adventurer recovered it.

**The sword has become central to Thunderaan's resurrection.** When the Earthen Ring decided to bring back the Prince of Air to fill the power vacuum left by Al'Akir's fall, they needed Thunderfury to accomplish this. The blade was brought to the Vortex Pinnacle and combined with the Heart of Skywall to fuel the resurrection during the Legion invasion. Its legendary status has made it one of WoW's most iconic weapons, even inspiring novelty items like an inflatable replica.

Cloud serpent mounts: Cloud serpent mounts are available in a variety of vibrant colors. When they were first added in Mists of Pandaria, they could only be used with the Cloud Serpent Riding skill, which required reputation with the Order of the Cloud Serpent and was not account-wide. With Cloud Serpent Riding's removal in patch 6.0.2, cloud serpents now have the same skill requirements as most other flying mounts—though they can still be time-consuming to acquire in the first place. All of these mounts are available to both factions. Reputation rewards The , , and can be purchased for each from San Redscale in the Jade Forest. You must be with the Order of the Cloud Serpent to purchase them. The can be purchased for from at the Shrine of Seven Stars or at the Shrine of Two Moons. You must be with the August Celestials to purchase this item. The can be purchased for 100,000 Timeless Coin from Mistweaver Ku on the Timeless Isle. You must be with Emperor Shaohao to purchase this item. Achievement rewards The is a reward from Glory of the Pandaria Hero. The is a reward from Glory of the Pandaria Raider. The can be purchased for from guild vendors after your guild has earned Guild Glory of the Pandaria Raider. You must be with your guild to purchase this item. The is a reward from Lord of the Reins. original.response:
The **Prideful Gladiator's Cloud Serpent** was an exclusive reward for top-ranking arena teams at the end of **Arena Season 15**. Since that season ended years ago, this mount is no longer obtainable through normal gameplay.

If you're looking to acquire gladiator mounts from current or recent seasons, you'd need to compete in arena PvP and rank highly enough with your team to earn the seasonal reward. However, the Prideful variant specifically is a legacy item from Mists of Pandaria and would only be available if Blizzard ever re-released it or made it available through other means—which they haven't done.

original.question: Where can I get the Raid Finder tier gear from Blackrock Foundry, and what item level is it?
original.reference: The Raid Finder tier gear from Blackrock Foundry can be obtained directly from bosses or through bonus rolls. It is item level 655 and comes in four sets based on armor type (cloth, leather, mail, and plate) rather than class-specific sets.
snippets: Blackrock Foundry set: The Blackrock Foundry set is the six-piece Raid Finder equivalent of the Tier 17 armor set, and is obtained from Blackrock Foundry. Rather than class-specific sets, there is one set for each of the four armor types. All four sets are item level 655, compared to 665/680/695 for normal/heroic/mythic Tier 17. There are no tokens, as the armor pieces drop directly from the boss (or are acquired via bonus rolls). Cloth: Leather: Mail: Plate: Sources Slot SourceHead BlackhandShoulder Operator ThogarChest Flamebender Ka'grazHands The Iron MaidensLegs The Blast FurnaceFeet Kromog Patch changes Category:Armor item sets Category:Blackrock Foundry sets

Blackrock Foundry: thumb|The Blackrock Foundry overlooking The Pit. Blackrock Foundry is a raid instance introduced in Warlords of Draenor. It has 10 bosses, and serves as the source of Tier 17 gear. The entrance is in Gorgrond, and the raid was open on February 3, 2015. Background Adventure Guide This foundry was the ancestral home of the Blackrock orcs, wherein master smiths smelted and worked the impossibly hard ore that is the clan's namesake. Now, massive giants captured and broken by the Thunderlord heat the great forges, flamebenders of the Burning Blade imbue the ore with an inner fire, and engineers shape the slag according to otherworldly schematics. Warlord Blackhand's foundry is the center of the Iron Horde's military might, churning out the weaponry that will be used to raze Azeroth. The foundry has a shipyard where the dreadnaughts of the Iron Horde are forged and assembled before being sent to the Iron Docks.Iron Docks#Adventure Guide After the fall of Blackhand, the Iron Horde under the new leader, , retreated from the foundry to Iron Docks and from there to Tanaan.We Need a Shipwright (Horde) It was still active as new munitions, cannons and supplies of parts for the Iron Reaver were being sent to Tanaan's .Hotter Than FelWar Is FelAll Fel Breaks Loose Encounters Slagworks Only the unnatural heat of the Foundry furnace can smelt the Blackrock ore, essential to the might of the Iron Horde.

Blackrock Foundry Spoils: __NOTOC__ The Blackrock Foundry Spoils is rewarded from level 100, item level 660 Garrison Missions. There are four missions at each difficulty tier, and there are four tiers total. Players will be offered a mission for their followers complete that is one tier higher than what they are currently running. That is, non-raiders and Raid Finder runners will win Normal (item level 670) loot, Normal mode raiders will win Heroic (item level 685) loot, Heroic mode raiders will win Mythic (item level 700) loot, and Mythic mode raiders will win Mythic loot and a bonus. These missions are unlocked once two Garrison followers reach item level 660. The missions have an eight-hour duration and a nearly week-long expiration timer. Patch changes Other versions Category:Mission rewards

Tier 17: Tier 17 is the armor set associated with Patch 6.0.2. It is obtained from Blackrock Foundry. All the tokens can be used immediately to gain the set item associated with the token. Unlike tier tokens used since The Burning Crusade, the item created can be used for any specialization since secondary attributes specific to certain roles (hit, expertise, dodge and parry) on gear has been removed with Warlords of Draenor. Attributes like bonus armor (for tanks) and spirit (for healers) can thus only be attained from necklaces, cloaks, bracers, belts, boots, rings and trinkets. Unlike tier sets in Cataclysm and Mists of Pandaria, there is no Raid Finder version of tier 17. Instead, there are four Blackrock Foundry sets that only drop in Raid Finder mode, one for each armor type. Details Each set consists of five pieces and has three ranks: normal (665), heroic (680) and Mythic (695). Slot Token Source C P V Head Kromog, Blackrock Foundry Shoulder Operator Thogar, Blackrock Foundry Chest Flamebender Ka'graz, Blackrock Foundry Hands The Iron Maidens, Blackrock Foundry Legs The Blast Furnace, Blackrock Foundry Sets Class Tier 17 set (665/680/695) Gallery Category:Class sets Tier 17 Tier 17

Crucible (Blackrock Foundry): The Crucible, also known as the Iron Crucible, is the main area of the Blackrock Foundry where Blackhand can be found. Its floors contain an arena where Blackhand pours crucibles full of molten slag into The Black Hand, a vehicle depot, and a giant anvil-like platform. There is no exit from the depths. Trivia During the beta, it was referred to as the Iron Crucible.
original.response:
# Blackrock Foundry Raid Finder Gear

The Raid Finder tier gear from Blackrock Foundry comes in the form of **four armor-type specific sets** rather than class-specific sets. These sets drop directly from bosses throughout the raid—no tokens involved. You'll find the pieces from these bosses:

- **Head** from Blackhand
- **Shoulders** from Operator Thogar
- **Chest** from Flamebender Ka'graz
- **Hands** from The Iron Maidens
- **Legs** from The Blast Furnace
- **Feet** from Kromog

All pieces are **item level 655**, which is notably lower than the normal/heroic/mythic Tier 17 options (665/680/695). You can also acquire these pieces through bonus rolls on top of direct drops.

original.response:
# Getting Field Marshal's Plate Helm for Transmog

You can't transmog the original **Field Marshal's Plate Helm** — that item is locked and non-transmogrifiable. However, Blizzard created a solution: the **Replica Field Marshal's Plate Helm**, which is a transmogrifiable version of the same appearance.

To get the Replica version, you need to purchase it from **Krixel Pinchwhistle in Netherstorm**. The catch is that you must have earned at least **Knight-Lieutenant rank** (or its legacy equivalent) in PvP to qualify. If you haven't earned that rank before, you'll need to earn it now through current PvP activities, then you'll be eligible to buy the replica and use it for transmog.

original.question: Where can I farm or find the Mammoth Helm in World of Warcraft?
original.reference: Mammoth Helm is a world drop in World of Warcraft. This means it can be obtained from defeating enemies anywhere in the game world, rather than from a specific location or boss.
snippets: Mammoth Helm: Mammoth Helm is a world drop. Patch changes External links Category:Wrath of the Lich King world drops

Mammoth: right|thumb|A mammoth of Northrend. thumb|A mammoth of the Dragon Isles with a calf. Mammoths are large, shaggy beasts found in Northrend and on the Dragon Isles that resemble behemoths or elekk. They can be found in the Borean Tundra, the Dragonblight, Sholazar Basin, and Zul'Drak in Northrend, and the Azure Span and Ohn'ahran Plains on the Dragon Isles. However, they are frequently hunted and slain by poachers, the most notable of which is Kaw the Mammoth Destroyer, a member of Hemet Nesingwary's hunting party. They are used as mounts by the frost giants of the Sons of Hodir. Background thumb|Wrath of the Lich King concept art.
Mammoth (Warcraft III): Mammoths are mammoth creeps that can be encountered in the Icecrown Glacier tileset. Gallery External links Category:Mammoths

Mammoth Bindings: Mammoth Bindings are a world drop. When disenchanted Dream Shard Infinite Dust Greater Cosmic Essence Lesser Cosmic Essence Patch changes External links Category:Wrath of the Lich King world drops

Mammoth Boots: Mammoth Boots are a world drop and are looted from Titanium Lockbox. When disenchanted Dream Shard Infinite Dust Greater Cosmic Essence Lesser Cosmic Essence Patch changes External links Category:Wrath of the Lich King world drops
original.response:
**Mammoth Helm is a world drop from Wrath of the Lich King content**, which means it can drop from enemies and creatures throughout that expansion's zones. You won't find it from a specific vendor or guaranteed location—instead, you'll need to farm mobs in Northrend areas where Wrath loot tables are still active.

Your best bet is to focus on **Northrend zones like Borean Tundra, Dragonblight, Sholazar Basin, or Zul'Drak**, where mammoths and other creatures roam. Kill enemies in these areas and you'll have a chance at this world drop. Keep in mind that world drops have relatively low rates, so this can take some grinding. You might also check the auction house—other players often list these drops there, which could be faster than farming yourself.

original.response:
The **Vengeance Blade** can roll a wide variety of random enchantments, each providing different stat bonuses. Here's what's possible:

The enchantments include options like **of the Elder** (+33 Stamina, +22 Intellect, +9 Mana Per 5 sec), **of the Whale** (+43 Stamina, +28 Spirit), **of the Owl** (+28 Intellect, +28 Spirit), and many others focused on different stat combinations. You can get pure stat rolls like **of Stamina** (+64 Stamina), **of Strength** (+43 Strength), or **of Agility** (+43 Agility), or mixed rolls like **of the Bandit** (+33 Stamina, +45 Attack Power, +22 Agility) and **of the Champion** (+33 Stamina, +22 Strength, +22 Defense Rating).

There are also enchantments catering to casters, such as **of the Sorcerer** (+33 Stamina, +26 Spell Damage and Healing, +22 Intellect) and **of Intellect** (+43 Intellect). The full range covers physical DPS, tanks, and healers/casters, so you had a decent chance of getting something useful for your class regardless of what dropped.

# Crafting the Honorable Combatant's Spellblade

To craft the **Honorable Combatant's Spellblade**, you need **Blacksmithing** at a skill level of 75. Once you have the recipe, you can make the weapon at any blacksmithing station.

You'll get the initial recipe from either **Leedan Gustaf** or **Ozgrom Ragefang**. These same vendors also sell the **higher rank recipes** — Rank 2 and Rank 3 — which reduce the materials required to craft the weapon, making it more efficient to produce multiple copies. The exact costs for upgrading to those ranks aren't fully listed in the available information, but they're both purchasable from the same vendors.

This is a **PvP reward** weapon, so it fits into the Honorable Combatant gear lineup available to players gearing up for competitive content.

# Getting the Tale-Teller's Staff

The **Tale-Teller's Staff** has several sources depending on your playstyle. Your best bet is **Class Hall missions** — it can drop as a reward from missions like A Fel Journey, Mind Your Step, Great Steaks, Shoot to Thrill, Traitors Below, or Critical Crops. If you're actively running those, keep an eye out.

You can also farm it through **world quests in the Broken Isles** — specifically poor elite world quests, which will have it as a potential reward. Additionally, **Kirin Tor Chests** and **Curious Wyrmtongue Caches** can contain it, so if you're opening those for other loot, you might get lucky. It's a random drop from all these sources, so persistence is key!

original.response:
**Bowyer Randolph** is an elite night elf archer located in the **Wintergrasp Fortress** in Northrend. He serves the Alliance during the Wintergrasp PvP battles.

He offers the quest **Bones and Arrows**, a weekly quest that sends you to the Forest of Shadows on the western edge of Wintergrasp. Your objective is to retrieve 10 Quivers of Dragonbone Arrows from Horde players in that area. According to Randolph, he's been equipping the Alliance archers with dragonbone arrowheads, but supplies have dried up since the massacre at Angrathar, so you'll need to collect bone splinters from the Forest of Shadows—including any the Horde have gathered.

Before his deployment to Northrend, Randolph served as **Commander Randolph** with the Stormpike Guard in Alterac Valley, where he was stationed at Stonehearth Bunker.

Their numbers are endless and their power is beyond reckoning, and Yor'sahj intends to reap a grim reward for his faithful service. Abilities Strategy Since the colors of the mounds in the environment correspond to the oozes that spawn from them, it may make coordinating the encounter easier by placing World Markers in front of the ooze spawn points. Beginners should use two tanks who take turns to let the Void Bolt stacks fall off. Raids with more powerful healers and tanks can use a single tank if they can survive eight or nine stacks of Void Bolt. When the oozes spawn, the raid leader can call out a single ooze so the raid can target and destroy it quickly. When one ooze dies, the remaining two become invulnerable and give the boss a buff or ability. original.response:
# Finding Quests and Trainers in Dun Morogh

**Coldridge Valley** is your starting zone if you're a new dwarf, located in the southwestern part of Dun Morogh. The quests there are designed to be straightforward and beginner-friendly, covering levels 1-6. If you're just starting out, the game will guide you through the opening quests naturally, so don't worry about finding them — they're right there to pick up.

For trainers, **Ironforge** is your hub. The dwarven city contains all your class and profession trainers. If you're leveling as a warrior specifically, you can find **Thran Khorman** at Anvilmar in Dun Morogh — he'll teach you the basics and offers quests like "Getting Battle-Ready" to help you develop your combat skills.

As you progress beyond the starter zone, **Dun Morogh contains numerous questgivers** spread throughout the region. There's also **Ultham Ironhorn** at Amberstill Ranch (east of Ironforge along the road) who offers riding training once you're ready to learn to ride. For a complete list of all available quests organized by level, check the Dun Morogh quests table — it'll show you exactly what's available at each stage of your leveling journey.

original.question: Who was Kelmarin in World of Warcraft lore and what role did he play during the Third War?
original.reference: Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner during the Third War. He was one of three scouts sent to investigate the Scourge army, and he distinguished himself by returning to inform Sylvanas of the approaching enemy forces while his fellow scouts waited. Sylvanas trusted him greatly, knowing his information was always accurate and detailed.
snippets: Kelmarin: Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner's command during the Third War. He was one of the three scouts sent to investigate the Scourge army, the other two being Sheldaris and Vor'athil. While the other two had waited for Sylvanas to come with the bulk of the rangers, he returned to Sylvanas to inform her about the approaching army. Sylvanas knew him well, and knew his information was always accurate and detailed.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, chapter 18 Quotes "Aye, my lady. I heard him called so by those who served him. I do not think the rumors painting him as the slayer of his father and the instigator of the troubles in Lordaeron are exaggerations, from what I have seen.""My lady, if he breaks through - I do not think we have the numbers to defeat him." original.response:
# Kelmarin: Sylvanas's Trusted Scout

**Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner during the Third War.** He was one of three scouts—alongside Sheldaris and Vor'athil—sent to investigate the approaching Scourge army. What set Kelmarin apart was his reliability and initiative. While his fellow scouts waited for Sylvanas to arrive with reinforcements, **Kelmarin made the decision to return and report directly to Sylvanas about the incoming threat.**

**Sylvanas trusted Kelmarin implicitly**, knowing his information was always accurate and detailed. This confidence in his intelligence-gathering abilities made him a valuable asset to her command during the conflict against the undead forces. His scouting reports helped Sylvanas understand the scale and nature of the danger she and her rangers faced, though his warnings about the overwhelming power of the Scourge proved grimly prophetic—he expressed doubt that even their numbers would be sufficient to defeat Arthas and his undead army.

original.question: What exactly are the jinyu in World of Warcraft and where do they come from?
original.reference: The jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures that live in Pandaria. They originated from murlocs who lived near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, where the magic expanded their minds and bodies until they became one of Pandaria's great ancient cultures. They are an elder and wise race known as masters of the water, worshipping the August Celestials in return for knowledge and guidance.
snippets: Jinyu: The jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures that live in Pandaria.Blizzcon 2011: World of Warcraft: Mists of Pandaria Revealed They are an elder and wise race, looked up to even by the pandaren. Masters of the water, they have been known to stick their staffs in rivers and literally hear what the land is saying. The waterspeakers are an upper caste, spiritual leaders that use the river to listen to the future. The ankoan are descended from jinyu who left Pandaria long ago to hunt in the depths of the oceans.Where the Road Leads - "A people called the jinyu aided the Alliance in Pandaria. Are they your kin?" "My people are known as the ankoan. We left the land behind long ago." History thumb|A typical jinyu in World of Warcraft. Early history The jinyu originated from murlocs who were fortunate enough to live near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The magic of the pools expanded their minds and grew their bodies until they became one of the great ancient cultures of Pandaria.Fish Tails They worshipped the August Celestials, and in return, the spirits offered them knowledge and guidance. For a time, there was peace.The Chronicle of Ages#Sheilun, Staff of the Mists The Temple of the Jade Serpent particularly later became sacred to the jinyu, who now use its scrying pool to serve as their eyes and ears throughout the continent. In the days before the Sundering, legend has it that all rivers flowed to Pandaria.

Jinyu Captive: Jinyu Captives are jinyu held in cages in Grookin Hill in in the Jade Forest. After the Horde trained the hozen into an army, the jinyu became practice targets - a Grookin Soldier leads them below the hill to the Dookin' Grounds where Grookin Grenadiers test their bomb-throwing skills on the captives, exploding. Quotes Gossip Lucky for you I'm in this cage. My people and their new allies will burn this village to the ground. I destroyed twenty hozen before my capture! Hozen! Your time is short. We have new allies! Dialogue Patch changes External links Category:Jinyu Category:Prisoners Category:Jade Forest NPCs

Jinyu empire: The jinyu empire was the empire of the jinyu on Pandaria. In the years prior to Lei Shen's rise to power, the wise jinyu had arrived at the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and established a small empire. When the Thunder King began to see all other races within his domain as servants, he launched a brutal campaign against the jinyu. Though they fought valiantly, the fish-men eventually crumbled before the might of Lei Shen.World of Warcraft: Chronicle Volume 1, pg. 82 During one battle, the mogu fought against the jinyu empire in a battle that lasted forty days and forty nights. Haqin of the Hundred Spears countered their attacks and defenses and won the battle for the mogu. Afterwards, the Thunder King presented Haqin with a hundred of the finest golden spears crafted by mogu forgemasters. He became one of Lei Shen's greatest lieutenants, and after his death, the spears were buried at his side.Haqin of the Hundred Spears On the eve of the jinyu's final stand against the mogu, they were betrayed by their allies, the hozen, who had secretly made a pact with Lei Shen. The hozen believed that in exchange for their loyalty to the Thunder King, they would receive preferential treatment under the mogu empire's reign (a promise that was never kept). This betrayal ensured the jinyu's defeat and created a vicious feud between the two races that would last for centuries. Pearlfin Many thousand years ago, the Pearlfin Tribe stood on the throne of all Pandaria.

Where Are They Coming From?: If we do not discover where they're coming from and how they're able to work so quickly, we'll be overrun before we can act in force. Search to the southeast and see if you can find the source of these skeletons. Progress Have you found the source of the skeletons? Completion I suspected such. We can't let this continue. Our first priority is to destroy those summoning altars and then deal with the mess the Scourge has already created here. Notes The altars are south of Blackwatch along the path to Corp'rethar at Use an altar to summon a Skeletal Constructor. Progression External links Category:Knights of the Ebon Blade quests

original.response:
# The Jinyu of World of Warcraft

The **jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures native to Pandaria**. They're an ancient and wise civilization that stands out as one of the great cultures of the continent—so respected that even the pandaren look up to them. The jinyu are masters of the water with unique spiritual abilities; their waterspeakers are an upper caste of spiritual leaders who can use rivers to literally listen to the land and perceive the future.

**Their origins trace back to murlocs** who lived near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The transformative magic of these sacred pools expanded their minds and grew their bodies, eventually elevating them from simple creatures into one of Pandaria's greatest civilizations. They came to worship the August Celestials, who in turn offered them knowledge and guidance. The Temple of the Jade Serpent became particularly sacred to them, with its scrying pool serving as their eyes and ears across the continent.

The jinyu's golden age didn't last forever. **They once ruled a great empire**, but their power was shattered by Lei Shen, the Thunder King. During his rise, he conquered them in a brutal campaign—a conflict made inevitable when their hozen allies betrayed them and sided with the mogu in exchange for promised rewards (rewards that never materialized). This ancient defeat created a centuries-long feud between the jinyu and hozen that would echo through Pandarian history.

# Der'izu Helm Farming and Stats

The **Der'izu Helm drops as a world drop from level 66-70 mobs**, so you'll want to farm enemies in that level range across Outland zones. There's no specific rare spawn or dungeon boss associated with it—just keep killing relevant leveled creatures and hope for the drop.

As for stats, the helm can roll with a huge variety of **random enchantments** depending on the suffix. You could get defensive options like +50 Stamina with various resistances, offensive stats like +100 Attack Power or +50 Strength/Intellect/Agility, healing-focused rolls with +110 Healing, or hybrid builds mixing multiple stats. Some notable rolls include **of the Bandit** (+39 Stamina, +52 Attack Power, +26 Agility), **of the Invoker** for casters (+26 Spell Critical Strike, +30 Spell Damage, +26 Intellect), or **of Stamina** if you just want a fat health pool (+75 Stamina). The randomization means you might need to farm multiple copies to get the exact roll you're after.

original.response:
# Greater Cache of Treasures Rewards

A **Greater Cache of Treasures** contains some solid loot when you complete scenarios. You'll get **1-3 Motes of Harmony** along with **60 Justice Points** as consistent rewards.

The cache also has a chance to drop **randomly enchanted gear** at item levels 450, 463, or 476, which scales based on the difficulty. Beyond the standard drops, you might snag **Brawler's Guild items**, a **Vial of Reddish Ooze**, or the transmog item **The Bear and the Lady Fair**.

You can earn these caches by completing any randomly-selected scenario, subsequent Heroic scenarios after your first one each day, or by finishing the two specific quests: *The King and the Council* and *The Warchief and the Darkness*.

clear_direct.question: Who is Arthas Menethil?
clear_direct.reference: Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and a Knight of the Silver Hand. He became a death knight after claiming the cursed runeblade Frostmourne in Northrend, sacrificing his soul in the process. He eventually became the Lich King after merging with Ner'zhul, and ruled from the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel until he was defeated by a group of adventurers.
snippets: Arthas Menethil: Arthas Menethil, Crown Prince of Lordaeron and Knight of the Silver Hand, was the son of King Terenas Menethil II and heir to the throne. He was trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and was inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand. Arthas also had a romantic relationship with the kind sorceress Jaina Proudmoore. Committed to the protection of his people, Arthas was determined to stop the plague spreading throughout Lordaeron. Despite his promising beginnings, Arthas became one of the most powerful and evil beings Azeroth would ever know. Although he killed the necromancer Kel'Thuzad, Arthas was unable to stop the spread of the plague and resorted to increasingly desperate methods, such as purging the city of Stratholme. Taking up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne, Arthas was able to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, but he lost his soul in the process. Becoming a death knight of the Scourge, he led the undead in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran. Arthas later made his way to the Frozen Throne of Icecrown and merged with the Lich King.March Badness Ruling as the Lich King for years afterward, Arthas had the last remnants of his humanity destroyed and was ultimately defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. Cradled by the spirit of his father, Arthas Menethil died, leaving the mantle of the Lich King to be taken by a noble soul who would contain the power of the Scourge.

Echo of Arthas Menethil: The Echo of Arthas Menethil is being tortured by the Echo of Ner'zhul inside Frostmourne during The Call of Icecrown. __NOTOC__ Abilities Quotes Trivia The Echo of Arthas Menethil is voiced by Patrick Seitz. clear_direct.response:
# Arthas Menethil

Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and one of World of Warcraft's most pivotal and tragic figures. A skilled paladin trained by Uther the Lightbringer and inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand, he was poised to be a noble ruler. He was romantically involved with Jaina Proudmoore and deeply committed to protecting his people from the plague ravaging Lordaeron.

However, Arthas's descent into darkness defines his legacy. Unable to stop the plague's spread through conventional means, he resorted to increasingly desperate and brutal methods, culminating in the purge of Stratholme. He took up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, but this victory cost him his soul—he became a death knight of the Scourge. Under the Lich King's control, Arthas led the undead forces that destroyed Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran.

Arthas eventually merged with the Lich King at the Frozen Throne in Icecrown, ruling as the Lich King for years with his remaining humanity stripped away. He was finally defeated by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. As he died, cradled by the spirit of his father King Terenas, the mantle of the Lich King passed to another.

clear_direct.question: What is Frostmourne?
clear_direct.reference: Frostmourne is a cursed runeblade created by the Lich King. It was used by Arthas Menethil after he claimed it in Northrend, which caused him to lose his soul and become a death knight. The sword has the power to steal the souls of those it kills. It was eventually shattered during the final battle against the Lich King in Icecrown Citadel.
snippets: Frostmourne: thumb|upright=0.8|The Lich King holding Frostmourne. Frostmourne was the mourneblade of the Lich King. A fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword,Blood of the Highborne, chapter 2 it possessed the power to shatterA Paladin's Soul and imprison the souls of its victims and could remake the living into mindless undead. Frostmourne was thrust from the Frozen Throne by the Lich King Ner'zhul and was taken up by Arthas Menethil in the wastes of Northrend during the Third War. It was wielded by Arthas both during his time as a death knight and as Lich King until it was destroyed by Tirion Fordring; in that time, the blade spilled the blood of kings, destroyed nations, and inflicted scars on the world that remain to this day. Few weapons have shaped modern history as much as Frostmourne.Libram of the Dead: Blades of the Fallen Prince During the third invasion of the Burning Legion, a death knight hero reforged the shards of the blade into the Blades of the Fallen Prince. History Origin thumb|A vision of Frostmourne in the hands of the Jailer. Frostmourne and the Helm of Domination were crafted at the Runecarver's Oubliette in Torghast by the Runecarver, who was forced against his will to do so by the Jailer.

Frostmourne Cavern is a cave located at the base of the Icecrown Glacier where the dark runeblade Frostmourne once lay on an altar, encased in ice, with the following inscription: "Whomsoever takes up this blade shall wield power eternal. Just as the blade rends flesh, so must power scar the spirit". The ice-rimmed cavern that extends back into the mountains is full of saronite.Exploring Azeroth: Northrend, pg. 91 The cavern was given its name by the Scarlet Onslaught.Frostmourne Cavern (quest) At the time of the Third War, its entrance was protected by a group of revenants led by one of them known as The Guardian.Frostmourne (WC3 Human) When Arthas Menethil came with Muradin Bronzebeard to claim the artifact in his quest of destroying the dreadlord Mal'Ganis, the Guardian ordered them to turn back, and when they refused, a fight took place and all the revenants were destroyed. In its final moments, the Guardian attempted to stop Arthas, explaining that he was not protecting the sword, but everyone from it. However, the prince didn't listen and claimed Frostmourne from its ice prison, which caused its explosion, and a piece of it "killed" Muradin.

clear_direct.response:
# Frostmourne

Frostmourne is the legendary runeblade of the Lich King—a fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword that stands as one of the most significant weapons in World of Warcraft's history. Crafted by the Runecarver at Torghast under the Jailer's coercion, the blade possesses immense dark power: it can shatter souls, imprison the spirits of its victims within itself, and transform the living into mindless undead.

The weapon was thrust from the Frozen Throne by Ner'zhul and claimed by Prince Arthas Menethil in Frostmourne Cavern during the Third War. Arthas wielded it throughout his transformation into a death knight and his eventual rise as the Lich King himself. During this time, Frostmourne spilled the blood of kings, destroyed entire nations, and left permanent scars on the world. The blade was ultimately destroyed by Tirion Fordring during the confrontation with the Lich King.

After its destruction, the shards of Frostmourne were reforged into the Blades of the Fallen Prince by a death knight hero during the third invasion of the Burning Legion, ensuring that even in pieces, this legendary weapon continued to influence the fate of Azeroth.

clear_direct.question: What is the Wrathgate?
clear_direct.reference: The Wrathgate, also known as Angrathar the Wrathgate, is a massive iron gate that serves as the entrance to Icecrown from the Dragonblight in Northrend. It was the site of a major battle where Horde and Alliance forces fought against the Scourge. The battle was interrupted when a faction of undead called the Forsaken Royal Apothecary Society unleashed a plague that killed both Scourge and Alliance and Horde forces, including High Overlord Saurfang the Younger.
snippets: Angrathar the Wrathgate: thumb|upright=1.1|Angrathar the Wrathgate. thumb|upright=1.1|After being cleaned by the red dragonflight. Angrathar the Wrathgate is a barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel and is located in the northwest of Dragonblight. It is a towering wall of knife edges made from saronite. During the war against the Lich King, the battle at the Wrath Gate was to be the first major blow against the Lich King, and the first in which the Alliance and Horde fought side by side instead of against each other. History Wrath of the Lich King thumb|Warlord Dranosh Saurfang and Highlord Bolvar Fordragon fighting the undead at the Wrathgate. During the war against the Lich King, the Wrath Gate was under siege by both the Alliance and the Horde forces, which set up the bases of Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard. Respectively led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang, their forces intended to defeat the Scourge forces at the gate and to storm into Icecrown Citadel. Their campaign culminated in the disastrous Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate, where they faced the Lich King himself, before being betrayed by Grand Apothecary Putress and his allies, who betrayed the Horde and all present by unleashing the New Plague on all the gathered forces. As the plague spread through the battlefield, many Horde and Alliance attempted to flee. The Lich King was also in serious danger of succumbing to the plague, and only barely managed to escape back through the Wrathgate.

Chronocrystal - Memory of the Wrathgate: thumb|Chronocrystal - Memory of the Wrathgate The Chronocrystal - Memory of the Wrathgate is a Chronocrystal located in the Caverns of Time next to Sa'at. This crystal plays The Wrathgate. Patch changes External links Category:Crystals Category:Caverns of Time objects

clear_direct.response:
# The Wrathgate

Angrathar the Wrathgate is a towering barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel located in northwestern Dragonblight. It's constructed from saronite and consists of knife-like edges forming a massive defensive wall.

During the Wrath of the Lich King expansion, the Wrathgate became the site of a historic battle where Alliance and Horde forces cooperated for the first time in a major campaign against the Lich King. Led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon (Alliance) and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang (Horde), their combined armies sieged the gate with the goal of breaching Icecrown Citadel.

However, the Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate ended in disaster when Grand Apothecary Putress betrayed both factions and unleashed the New Plague on all gathered forces. The biological weapon devastated the battlefield, forcing both sides to retreat. Even the Lich King himself was threatened by the plague and barely escaped back through the gate he controlled. This catastrophic event became a turning point in the war against the Scourge, demonstrating the dangers of the plague weapons being developed.
