clear_direct.question: Hey, I'm doing Terrace of Endless Spring for the first time. What's the deal with Lei Shi? Is she hard and what should our raid know before we pull her?
clear_direct.reference: Lei Shi is the third boss in Terrace of Endless Spring and involves challenging tank swap mechanics with strong adds that revive periodically. Your raid should position in a triangle formation with tanks on separate corners to minimize Spray damage, and be prepared for frequent tank switches when she casts Protect at 80%, 60%, 40%, and 20% health. The main challenge is managing the reviving Corrupted Protectors while handling the tank swap mechanics.
snippets: Lei Shi: Lei Shi is the third boss in Terrace of Endless Spring. She is a sha-corrupted water elemental who will help players with the final battle with the Sha of Fear after being freed from corruption. The fight involves challenging tank swap mechanics and strong adds that revive periodically. Once the sha is defeated, her image appears on the main platform. She spawns by defeating the Corrupted Protectors, she ripples nervously upon their defeat, after the player causes AOE damage it will make her ripple to the point it becomes fully visible. Background Adventure Guide For countless years, this friendly water spirit frolicked in the Fountains of Endless Spring with childlike wonder and glee. When fighting suddenly broke out in this peaceful place, Lei Shi saw the crystal-clear water turn cloudy and murky, and terror fell over her mind. Spells and abilities Notes Strategy Position the raid in a triangle, with one tank on one corner, the second tank on another corner and the remainder of the raid on the third corner (similar to the positioning for Phase 2 of Beth'tilac in the Firelands). The aim of this positioning is to have only the active tank affected by Spray. The two tanks will regularly need to taunt off each other to prevent Spray from stacking too high. When Lei Shi casts Protect (at 80%, 60%, 40% and 20%), perform a tank switch, and have the off-tank pick up the s (Lei Shi continues to cast Spray while the Protectors are up).

Terrace of Endless Spring: The Terrace of Endless Spring (or the Terrace of Eternal Spring)The Emperor's Gift is one of the three launch raids of World of Warcraft: Mists of Pandaria. It allows players to finally defeat the Sha of Fear, a "super super huge raid boss" who "has turned other local creatures into Sha",https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YtEsFzry5yc after driving it out of the Heart of Fear. The raid is relatively small with four boss encounters: the Protectors of the Endless, a trio of Jinyu; Tsulong, a cloud serpent; Lei Shi, a water spirit; and the Sha of Fear itself as the final boss. The raid is located at the midpoint of the Veiled Stair, a mountain pass between the northeastern corner of the Valley of the Four Winds and the southern part of the Kun-Lai Summit. The meeting stone and a flight path are located just down a short hill from the raid dungeon entrance, near the Tavern in the Mists deep within the Stair. Normal mode opened November 13, 2012. Raid Finder and Heroic modes opened on November 20, 2012. Raid Finder difficulty requires an average item level of 470.

clear_direct.response:
# Lei Shi Strategy Guide

Lei Shi is the third boss of Terrace of Endless Spring and features **strong tank swap mechanics** as her core mechanic. She's a sha-corrupted water elemental who casts **Spray**, a stacking debuff that the active tank must manage by regularly taunting off to the off-tank to prevent the stacks from getting too dangerous.

## Key Mechanics

The fight has periodic **Protect phases at 80%, 60%, 40%, and 20% health**, where Lei Shi summons adds that must be picked up by the off-tank while the main tank continues tanking her. Your raid will also deal with **reviving adds** that spawn periodically throughout the encounter.

## Positioning

Position your raid in a **triangle formation**: place one tank at one corner, the second tank at another corner, and the rest of the raid (DPS and healers) at the third corner. This positioning ensures only the active tank gets hit by Spray, making it much easier to manage the stacking debuff. The tanks need to communicate well and swap aggro consistently to keep Spray from becoming overwhelming.

The good news: once you defeat Lei Shi, she'll actually help you in the final fight against the Sha of Fear itself, so you're working toward freeing her from corruption rather than just killing her.

clear_direct.question: How is Freezing Arrow different from just laying a regular Freezing Trap, and when would I want to use it instead?
clear_direct.reference: Freezing Arrow allows hunters to place a Freezing Trap at a distance using a targeting reticule, rather than having to be in melee range to lay a regular trap. This is particularly useful because it doesn't require the mob to be targeted or in focus, enabling hunters to crowd control ranged mobs, re-establish CC when a previous trap breaks, and chain-trap more flexibly without disengaging from their main target.
snippets: Freezing Arrow: Freezing Arrow was a Marksmanship hunter honor talent, available at rank 43. Notes This ability is essentially a ranged version of Freezing Trap for Marksmanship, with some key differences: the disorient effect also applies to nearby enemies, it doesn't have an arming delay, and can be used from a distance. Tips and tactics When in Arena, the Freezing Arrow is extremely useful, since casters who have to stand in one place will always fall prey to its icy grip. Even for classes who are mobile, you can still attempt to predict their path of travel and intercept them with a freezing arrow. This method is especially useful for paladins, who will likely blow both their Divine Shield and Honorable Medallion to escape its grip. Patch changes External links Category:Freezes

Freezing Arrow (hunter ability): Freezing Arrow is a hunter ability which can place a Freezing Trap at a distance. Notes This ability is essentially a marriage of the Flare and Freezing Trap abilities. Upon activating the ability, you are presented with a targeting reticule that shows the placement and radius of your trap. Once fired, it can take between one and two seconds to land, and an additional one second for the trap to arm. The trap acts exactly like a trap laid by Freezing Trap. This shares a cooldown with other trap abilities and Black Arrow. Improvements (See Freezing Trap for improvements.) Tips and tactics thumbnail|left|Troll Hunter firing a Freezing Arrow at a Void Summoner in Aldur'thar While the utility of this ability is limited against other players, it excels in group or solo questing, and is marginally useful in instances and raids. Some specific situations where it can prove useful are as follows. Against groups that have a ranged DPS mob, the hunter can place the trap to crowd control the ranged mob, which allows a pet or group member to aggro melee mobs without fear. When a mob breaks a previous trap or crowd control unexpectedly, the hunter may fire the Arrow as desired, to re-establish CC, without disengaging from the main target being killed. This is because, unlike many other crowd-control abilities, Freezing Arrow does not require having the mob as a target or focus. This also enables more flexible chain-trapping (see Freezing Trap for more details).

Freezing Trap: thumb|In the TCG Freezing Trap is a level 10 hunter ability. This trap offers long-duration crowd control that breaks on damage. Hunters who start in Exile's Reach unlock this by completing A Hunter's Trap. Notes and trivia Freezing Trap has a PvP duration of 8 seconds and is subject to diminishing returns. Immunities such as Ice Block and Divine Shield can break Freezing Trap's effect. Using Tame Beast on a frozen target will not break the effect. Freezing Trap is a common Hunter spell in Hearthstone. The flavor text reads: "'Dang, that's cold.' - appropriate response to Freezing Trap, or a mean joke." clear_direct.response:
# Freezing Arrow vs. Freezing Trap

Freezing Arrow is a ranged version of Freezing Trap that can be cast from a distance with a targeting reticule, whereas Freezing Trap must be laid in place. The key differences are:

**Mechanical Advantages:**
- Fires from range without requiring you to move to the trap location
- No arming delay (instant cast vs. the 1-2 second landing time plus 1 second arming of Freezing Arrow)
- The disorient effect applies to nearby enemies in addition to the initial target
- Doesn't require having the mob targeted or focused to place it

**When to Use Freezing Arrow:**
Use it for flexible crowd control without disengaging from your current target. It excels when a mob breaks previous crowd control unexpectedly and you need to re-establish CC while continuing to attack your main target. It's also superior for chain-trapping since you can place traps reactively without repositioning.

In PvP (Arena specifically), Freezing Arrow is extremely useful against casters who must stand still and against mobile classes where you can predict their movement path and intercept them.

Both abilities share a cooldown with other trap abilities and Black Arrow, so you're choosing positioning strategy rather than having access to more frequent traps. Freezing Arrow trades the guaranteed placement of a pre-laid trap for the flexibility and range of reactive casting.

clear_direct.response:
Yes, you can safely kill Bloodmaul Brutes without damaging your Ogri'la reputation. They remain attackable even after you become neutral with Ogri'la, and killing them won't affect your reputation standing with that faction.

This applies to all the Bloodmaul ogres in Blade's Edge Mountains — the Brutes, Soothsayers, Geomancers, and Warlocks all have this same property. You can farm them freely while working toward Ogri'la reputation without any concerns about standing penalties.

# Zandalari Bladeguards

Zandalari Bladeguards are found in **Dazar'alor** and **Zuldazar**, with additional appearances in **Warport Rastari** and **Xibala** during the Alliance War Campaign.

## Abilities

Zandalari Bladeguards use the following abilities in combat:

- **Rending Cut** - A melee attack ability
- **Mortal Strike** - Inflicts physical damage and wounds the target, reducing healing received by 25% for 10 seconds
- **Regeneration** - Heals the caster every 2 seconds for 6 seconds

These guards serve as part of the Zandalari Empire's military forces and appear as enemies during the faction conflict in Battle for Azeroth content.

clear_direct.question: What dropped from Onyxia?
clear_direct.reference: Onyxia is a black dragon who is the daughter of Deathwing. She dropped tier 2 headpieces for all classes, the Onyxia Scale Cloak, and had a chance to drop the Deathbringer sword and the Vis'kag the Bloodletter. She was also known for dropping a very rare mount called Onyxia's Lair.
snippets: Onyxia: Onyxia serves as one of the two secondary antagonists (alongside her brother Nefarian) in classic World of Warcraft and one of the supporting antagonists in Cataclysm. Biography Chronormu's visage thumb|Onyxia in Hearthstone. Onyxia believed that humans were the mortal race that posed the greatest threat to dragonkind. When deciding on her mortal guise, she chose the form of an attractive female human so that she could manipulate their kind and allow her to seize all she wanted from them. At some point, the bronze dragon Chronormu—who could not make up his mind on what mortal form to choose during his upcoming Visage Day ceremony—sought an audience with Onyxia in the caverns of the Obsidian Dragonshrine, where the daughter of Deathwing was overseeing drakonid laborers and sparring drakes. Onyxia scoffed at Chronormu's view that dragons chose a visage to better relate to the mortal races, stating that it sounded "like something Nozdormu would teach his whelps". She assumed her human form and continued that no visage could change a dragon's nature as a hunter and killer. Like their other abilities, shapeshifting was a tool to take what they wanted from the young races. Though Chronormu protested that this was not how he wanted mortals to see him, Onyxia offered to let him stay by her side and teach him everything he needed to know about mortals and allow him to become the greatest member of his flight, great enough that even Nozdormu would kneel before him.

Head of Onyxia: thumb|Onyxia's Head Mounted in Stormwind Head of Onyxia begins the quest A Most Puzzling Circumstance. Source This item drops from Onyxia in Onyxia's Lair. Only one head drops per kill. Notes As of Patch 3.2.2, turning in this quest no longer gives the city-wide buff, Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer, as it did in the original level 60 quest. Patch changes Category:Onyxia's Lair items Category:Heads

Onyxia (tactics): Onyxia is a level ?? black dragon boss in Onyxia's Lair. Abilities Phase One Onyxia melees for about 12,000-15,000 on plate. Onyxia is tauntable. Phase Two thumb|Deep Breath IS random! Onyxia takes off at 65%, has no threat table, cannot be attacked from melee, and uses the following abilities: Phase Three Onyxia lands at 40% HP. She regains all her phase one abilities and gains some additional abilities. Spawn Onyxian Whelps - Small numbers of whelps will continue to spawn in phase 3. Strategy frame|Map of Onyxia's lair, showing raid positioning for Phases 1 & 3This is (still) a very simple encounter overall. For strategy videos of both the 10-man and 25-man encounter, look below the loot table on this page. You ideally want 2 tanks, 2 healers, 3 melee DPS, and 3 ranged DPS for 10 man, and 2 tanks, 5 healers, 8 melee DPS, and 10 ranged DPS for 25 man. Phase 1 The tank should run slightly ahead of the rest of the raid and grab Onyxia, quickly turning her away from the raid and then backing off to the Northern wall, where the tank will position themselves with their back to the wall to minimise the effect of the knockback from Wing Buffet. Only the tank should be in front of Onyxia, to minimize damage from fire breath and cleaving. During phase one, the only damage to the raid should be upon the tank. Ranged DPS and healers need to stay to either side of her, fairly far back.

Spawn of Onyxia: __NOTOC__ Spawn of Onyxia are black whelps located in the Wyrmbog in Dustwallow Marsh. Onyxia's Lair: Onyxia's Lair is a level 80 raid dungeon located in the Wyrmbog, Dustwallow Marsh. It is home to Onyxia, the brood mother of the black dragonflight. Onyxia's Lair was originally a level 60, 40-player raid dungeon, one of two that were available upon release of World of Warcraft (the other being Molten Core). It was re-tuned as a level 80 raid dungeon in patch 3.2.2 in honor of World of Warcraft's 5-year anniversary. Like all other Wrath of the Lich King raid dungeons, it has both 10-man and 25-man modes. Lore Onyxia built the lair. The entrance to the lair was carved to look like the maw of the broodmother herself, with its inner pathway being magma-lined and the ground cracked, glowing with fire from beneath. The arching stone ceiling groans and shifts, creating constant rockfalls. The walls spill lava where the heat can't be contained.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 148 Onyxia is the daughter of the mighty Black Aspect, Deathwing, and sister of the scheming Nefarian, Lord of Blackrock Spire. Whereas her father favors brute force, and her brother creates twisted abominations to carry out his will, Onyxia prefers subtle manipulation, and delights in meddling in the political affairs of mortals, using her charm and power to influence such delicate matters between the different races to her own ends. Some believe that Onyxia has even assumed an alias once used by her father - the title of the royal House Prestor.
clear_direct.response:
# What Dropped from Onyxia

**Onyxia's Head** was a notable drop from defeating Onyxia in Onyxia's Lair. Only one head dropped per kill, and it was used to begin the quest "A Most Puzzling Circumstance."

In the original level 60 raid, turning in Onyxia's Head granted a city-wide buff called **Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer**, though this reward was removed as of Patch 3.2.2 when the encounter was re-tuned for level 80 players.

clear_direct.question: What is Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker?
clear_direct.reference: Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is a legendary one-handed sword from vanilla WoW. It required players to collect two bindings that dropped from Garr and Baron Geddon in Molten Core, then complete a quest chain involving Highlord Demitrian. The weapon is known for its powerful proc that reduces enemy threat generation and chains lightning to nearby enemies. It became a famous meme in the WoW community.
snippets: Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker: thumb|Thunderfury's owner, Wray. Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is the legendary sword once wielded by Prince Thunderaan, Prince of Air. Equipping the sword awards the Feat of Strength, Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (achievement). It is currently the only legendary weapon that is available to all classes. History Ragnaros the Firelord, and his lieutenants Garr and Baron Geddon, defeated Thunderaan, Prince of Air, after the First Age of Creation, during the Elemental Sundering. Ragnaros was unable to fully consume Thunderaan’s essence, so he forced what little remained into a talisman of elemental binding and shattered it. He gave the two halves to his lieutenants. Ages passed, and somehow the talisman halves found their way into the hands of Highlord Demitrian, a follower of Thunderaan. He recombined the talisman but was unable to release his master. Instead, he did the best he could: He crafted a blade to serve as a vessel for his master's essence. Nik the Mountain can be seen wielding the blade. When the Earthen Ring decides to resurrect Thunderaan to replace the fallen Al'Akir, a shaman adventurer takes Thunderfury from the corpse of its last wielder — a dwarf warrior called Wray who died while facing RagemawBlessed Blade of the Windseeker — in Azsuna and brings it to the Vortex Pinnacle.

Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (quest item): Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is formed through combining Damaged Thunderfury with the Heart of Skywall. Its power was used to bring Thunderaan back during the third invasion of the Burning Legion.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 107 Objective of Recharging the Blade Patch changes References External links

During WoW's 12th Anniversary, it could be purchased from in Stormwind City or in Orgrimmar at a cost of . When used, it equips the player who uses it with a replica of the infamous Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker. It also rewards the achievement Did Someone Say...? the first time it is used. Blessed Blade of the Windseeker: Objectives Locate the lost blade of the Windseeker. Thunderfury located Damaged Thunderfury Description So the Earthen Ring wishes to resurrect my master? I knew this day would come! At last, humanity understands the power of the Windseeker! clear_direct.response:
# Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker

Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is a legendary sword that once belonged to Prince Thunderaan, Prince of Air. The weapon serves as a vessel for Thunderaan's essence after he was defeated and destroyed by Ragnaros the Firelord during the Elemental Sundering. Highlord Demitrian, a follower of Thunderaan, recombined the shattered talisman containing his master's remnants and forged it into this legendary blade.

The sword is notably the only legendary weapon available to all classes in World of Warcraft. Equipping it awards players a Feat of Strength achievement. Throughout the game's history, Thunderfury has been wielded by various characters and has played a crucial role in major events—most notably in resurrecting Thunderaan during the third invasion of the Burning Legion, when the Earthen Ring decided he should replace the fallen Al'Akir.

The blade's iconic status in WoW culture is so significant that Blizzard even created novelty items based on it, including an inflatable version available during the game's anniversary celebrations. The quest chain to obtain the legendary sword requires players to locate the damaged blade in Azsuna and restore its power using the Heart of Skywall.

Blackrock Foundry: thumb|The Blackrock Foundry overlooking The Pit. Blackrock Foundry is a raid instance introduced in Warlords of Draenor. It has 10 bosses, and serves as the source of Tier 17 gear. The entrance is in Gorgrond, and the raid was open on February 3, 2015. Background Adventure Guide This foundry was the ancestral home of the Blackrock orcs, wherein master smiths smelted and worked the impossibly hard ore that is the clan's namesake. Now, massive giants captured and broken by the Thunderlord heat the great forges, flamebenders of the Burning Blade imbue the ore with an inner fire, and engineers shape the slag according to otherworldly schematics. Warlord Blackhand's foundry is the center of the Iron Horde's military might, churning out the weaponry that will be used to raze Azeroth. The foundry has a shipyard where the dreadnaughts of the Iron Horde are forged and assembled before being sent to the Iron Docks.Iron Docks#Adventure Guide After the fall of Blackhand, the Iron Horde under the new leader, , retreated from the foundry to Iron Docks and from there to Tanaan.We Need a Shipwright (Horde) It was still active as new munitions, cannons and supplies of parts for the Iron Reaver were being sent to Tanaan's .Hotter Than FelWar Is FelAll Fel Breaks Loose Encounters Slagworks Only the unnatural heat of the Foundry furnace can smelt the Blackrock ore, essential to the might of the Iron Horde.

Blackrock Foundry Spoils: __NOTOC__ The Blackrock Foundry Spoils is rewarded from level 100, item level 660 Garrison Missions. There are four missions at each difficulty tier, and there are four tiers total. Players will be offered a mission for their followers complete that is one tier higher than what they are currently running. That is, non-raiders and Raid Finder runners will win Normal (item level 670) loot, Normal mode raiders will win Heroic (item level 685) loot, Heroic mode raiders will win Mythic (item level 700) loot, and Mythic mode raiders will win Mythic loot and a bonus. These missions are unlocked once two Garrison followers reach item level 660. The missions have an eight-hour duration and a nearly week-long expiration timer. Patch changes Other versions Category:Mission rewards

Tier 17: Tier 17 is the armor set associated with Patch 6.0.2. It is obtained from Blackrock Foundry. All the tokens can be used immediately to gain the set item associated with the token. Unlike tier tokens used since The Burning Crusade, the item created can be used for any specialization since secondary attributes specific to certain roles (hit, expertise, dodge and parry) on gear has been removed with Warlords of Draenor. Attributes like bonus armor (for tanks) and spirit (for healers) can thus only be attained from necklaces, cloaks, bracers, belts, boots, rings and trinkets. Unlike tier sets in Cataclysm and Mists of Pandaria, there is no Raid Finder version of tier 17. Instead, there are four Blackrock Foundry sets that only drop in Raid Finder mode, one for each armor type. Details Each set consists of five pieces and has three ranks: normal (665), heroic (680) and Mythic (695). Crucible (Blackrock Foundry): The Crucible, also known as the Iron Crucible, is the main area of the Blackrock Foundry where Blackhand can be found. Its floors contain an arena where Blackhand pours crucibles full of molten slag into The Black Hand, a vehicle depot, and a giant anvil-like platform. There is no exit from the depths. Trivia During the beta, it was referred to as the Iron Crucible.
clear_direct.response:
# Blackrock Foundry Raid Finder Gear

The Raid Finder tier gear from Blackrock Foundry comes as the **Blackrock Foundry set**, a six-piece armor set that drops directly from bosses (or through bonus rolls). There are no tier tokens—instead, you get one set for each of the four armor types (cloth, leather, mail, and plate).

The item level is **655**, which is lower than the normal/heroic/mythic versions at 665/680/695 respectively.

**Gear drops from specific bosses:**
- Head: Blackhand
- Shoulders: Operator Thogar
- Chest: Flamebender Ka'graz
- Hands: The Iron Maidens
- Legs: The Blast Furnace
- Feet: Kromog

# Getting Field Marshal's Plate Helm for Transmog

You cannot transmog the original Field Marshal's Plate Helm—it's not transmogrifiable. However, you can use the **Replica Field Marshal's Plate Helm** for transmog purposes instead.

To obtain the replica, purchase it from **Krixel Pinchwhistle in Netherstorm**. You'll need to have earned at least **Knight-Lieutenant** (or its legacy version, Knight-Lieutenant Legacy) PvP rank to qualify for the purchase. This means you must have done PvP to achieve that rank at some point, even if it was years ago.

The replica has the same appearance as the original Field Marshal's Plate Helm and is fully transmogrifiable, so you can apply it to your current gear for the classic look.

Mammoths are large, shaggy beasts found in Northrend and on the Dragon Isles that resemble behemoths or elekk. They can be found in the Borean Tundra, the Dragonblight, Sholazar Basin, and Zul'Drak in Northrend, and the Azure Span and Ohn'ahran Plains on the Dragon Isles. # Crafting the Honorable Combatant's Spellblade

The **Honorable Combatant's Spellblade** is a blacksmithing recipe that requires a blacksmithing skill of 75 to craft. You'll create it through the Blacksmithing profession once you have the recipe.

## Getting the Recipe

The recipe is purchased from either **Leedan Gustaf** or **Ozgrom Ragefang**. Higher rank versions of the recipe (Rank 2 and Rank 3) are also available from these same vendors and reduce the materials required to craft the weapon.

The weapon itself is classified as a PvP reward, so expect it to be tied to PvP-related currency or vendors.

# Getting the Tale-Teller's Staff

The **Tale-Teller's Staff** can be obtained through several methods:

**Class Hall Missions** are your primary source. Complete any of these missions for a chance to receive it as a reward:
- A Fel Journey
- Mind Your Step
- Great Steaks
- Shoot to Thrill
- Traitors Below
- Critical Crops

**Other sources** include poor elite world quests in the Broken Isles, as well as loot from **Kirin Tor Chests** and **Curious Wyrmtongue Caches** found throughout the zone.

Since this is a Legion-era item, you'll need to be running content from that expansion to farm it effectively. Your best bet is running the Class Hall missions repeatedly until it drops, or farming world quests and opening the mentioned cache items.

# Bowyer Randolph Location and Quests

**Location:** Bowyer Randolph is found in the **Wintergrasp Fortress** in Northrend. He's an elite night elf serving the Alliance.

**Quest:** He offers the weekly quest **Bones and Arrows**, which tasks you with retrieving 10 Quivers of Dragonbone Arrows from Horde players in the Forest of Shadows (on the western edge of Wintergrasp). The quest involves slaying Horde players who are collecting bone splinters in that area for dragonbone arrowheads.

Before his assignment to Northrend, Randolph served as Commander Randolph with the Stormpike Guard in Alterac Valley's Stonehearth Bunker.

Yor'sahj the Unsleeping: thumb|Yor'sahj the Unsleeping. Yor'sahj the Unsleeping is the third boss (or second if killed before Warlord Zon'ozz) to appear in the Dragon Soul raid.http://us.battle.net/wow/en/blog/3516572 World first heroic kill on 25-man achieved 7.12.2011 by Blood Legion - US-Illidan. Background Adventure Guide Since the fall of the Bastion of Twilight, Yor'sahj the Unsleeping has eagerly assisted Deathwing, providing the Destroyer with the means to release more faceless ones from their prisons deep beneath the earth. Their numbers are endless and their power is beyond reckoning, and Yor'sahj intends to reap a grim reward for his faithful service. Abilities Strategy Since the colors of the mounds in the environment correspond to the oozes that spawn from them, it may make coordinating the encounter easier by placing World Markers in front of the ooze spawn points. Beginners should use two tanks who take turns to let the Void Bolt stacks fall off. Raids with more powerful healers and tanks can use a single tank if they can survive eight or nine stacks of Void Bolt. When the oozes spawn, the raid leader can call out a single ooze so the raid can target and destroy it quickly. When one ooze dies, the remaining two become invulnerable and give the boss a buff or ability. Globules by Color Black – Dark Globule Yor'sahj spawns a large number of untauntable Forgotten Ones, which fixate on raid members.

Dark Globule: Yor'sahj the Unsleeping calls to the might of Shu'ma, summoning three differently colored globules of his blood. These globules path slowly towards Yor'sahj, infusing him with additional powers if they reach him. When a Dark Globule reaches Yor'sahj, the corruption of the blood seeps out into the Maw of Shu'ma. This vile corruption then periodically creates Forgotten Ones that attack the players. Dun Morogh: This peaceful region is now threatened by the troggs who have begun appearing all over the area. During the Scourge Invasion, Dun Morogh was attacked by the Scourge. Due to the Cataclysm, the threats of the troggs and ice trolls have increased. Legion The Burning Legion targeted Dun Morogh as one of their invasion points. It was successfully defended by the Alliance, Horde and Illidari. Geography Dun Morogh contains no raid dungeons, micro dungeons, or battlegrounds. Gnomeregan, an instanced dungeon for adventurers levels 28 to 35, can be found in the western part of the zone, beyond Brewnall Village. Dun Morogh is the starting area for the dwarves and gnomes, and so it contains a very sheltered 1-5 leveling area - Coldridge Valley. The dwarven city, Ironforge, can also be found in this zone and is one of the major Alliance trade and travel hubs. # Dun Morogh Resources for Low-Level Dwarves

**Starting Zone**: Coldridge Valley (levels 1-6) is the designated starting area for dwarves and gnomes in the southwestern part of Dun Morogh. This sheltered zone contains beginner-friendly quests with in-game guidance to help you get started.

**Class Trainers**: Dun Morogh has multiple class trainers throughout the zone. For warriors specifically, Thran Khorman is located in Anvilmar and offers training quests like "Simple Rune" and "Getting Battle-Ready." Other class trainers are available for different classes.

**Profession Trainers**: The zone also contains trainers for professions and tradeskills, allowing you to develop gathering and crafting skills alongside your combat training.

**Quest Progression**: Beyond Coldridge Valley, numerous questgivers are scattered throughout Dun Morogh, with quests arranged by level. A dedicated questing guide exists that shows the most efficient order to complete all available quests in the zone. For specific quest locations and NPCs, consult the Dun Morogh quest tables organized by level.

**Major Hub**: Ironforge, the dwarven city located in this zone, serves as a major Alliance trade and travel hub where you can find vendors, additional trainers, and services.

clear_direct.question: Who was Kelmarin in World of Warcraft lore and what role did he play during the Third War?
clear_direct.reference: Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner during the Third War. He was one of three scouts sent to investigate the Scourge army, and he distinguished himself by returning to inform Sylvanas of the approaching enemy forces while his fellow scouts waited. Sylvanas trusted him greatly, knowing his information was always accurate and detailed.
snippets: Kelmarin: Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner's command during the Third War. He was one of the three scouts sent to investigate the Scourge army, the other two being Sheldaris and Vor'athil. While the other two had waited for Sylvanas to come with the bulk of the rangers, he returned to Sylvanas to inform her about the approaching army. Sylvanas knew him well, and knew his information was always accurate and detailed.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, chapter 18 Quotes "Aye, my lady. I heard him called so by those who served him. I do not think the rumors painting him as the slayer of his father and the instigator of the troubles in Lordaeron are exaggerations, from what I have seen.""My lady, if he breaks through - I do not think we have the numbers to defeat him." References Category:High elf characters Category:Scouts Category:Elven rangers Category:Farstriders Category:Lore characters Category:Arthas: Rise of the Lich King characters

Vor'athil: Vor'athil was a black-haired high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner's command during the Third War. He was one of the three scouts sent to investigate the Scourge army, the other two being Kelmarin and Sheldaris. While Kelmarin returned to Sylvanas to inform her about the approaching army, he and Sheldaris had waited for Sylvanas to come with the bulk of the rangers. He was killed by a gargoyle during the first struggle with the Scourge, and later raised as an undead. He had his long black hair swept back in a ponytail, tanned skin, pointed ears and eyes blue as summer skies.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, chapter 18 References Category:High elf characters Category:Darkfallen characters Category:Elven rangers Category:Scouts Category:Farstriders Category:Scourge Category:Lore characters Category:Arthas: Rise of the Lich King characters Category:World of Warcraft: Sylvanas characters

# Kelmarin in World of Warcraft Lore

Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner's command during the Third War. He was one of three scouts sent to investigate the approaching Scourge army, alongside Sheldaris and Vor'athil. While his fellow scouts waited for Sylvanas to arrive with the main ranger force, Kelmarin returned to report his findings to the ranger commander.

Sylvanas held Kelmarin in high regard, trusting his intelligence completely because his information was always accurate and detailed. His warnings about the Scourge threat were grave—he informed her that if Arthas broke through their defensive lines, the elves lacked the numbers to defeat him. Kelmarin's reconnaissance and urgent reports made him a crucial figure in Sylvanas's understanding of the dire threat posed by the undead army during the early stages of the Third War's assault on Lordaeron.

clear_direct.question: What exactly are the jinyu in World of Warcraft and where do they come from?
clear_direct.reference: The jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures that live in Pandaria. They originated from murlocs who lived near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, where the magic expanded their minds and bodies until they became one of Pandaria's great ancient cultures. They are an elder and wise race known as masters of the water, worshipping the August Celestials in return for knowledge and guidance.
snippets: Jinyu: The jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures that live in Pandaria.Blizzcon 2011: World of Warcraft: Mists of Pandaria Revealed They are an elder and wise race, looked up to even by the pandaren. Masters of the water, they have been known to stick their staffs in rivers and literally hear what the land is saying. The waterspeakers are an upper caste, spiritual leaders that use the river to listen to the future. The ankoan are descended from jinyu who left Pandaria long ago to hunt in the depths of the oceans.Where the Road Leads - "A people called the jinyu aided the Alliance in Pandaria. Are they your kin?" "My people are known as the ankoan. We left the land behind long ago." History thumb|A typical jinyu in World of Warcraft. Early history The jinyu originated from murlocs who were fortunate enough to live near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The magic of the pools expanded their minds and grew their bodies until they became one of the great ancient cultures of Pandaria.Fish Tails They worshipped the August Celestials, and in return, the spirits offered them knowledge and guidance. For a time, there was peace.The Chronicle of Ages#Sheilun, Staff of the Mists The Temple of the Jade Serpent particularly later became sacred to the jinyu, who now use its scrying pool to serve as their eyes and ears throughout the continent. In the days before the Sundering, legend has it that all rivers flowed to Pandaria.

Jinyu Captive: Jinyu Captives are jinyu held in cages in Grookin Hill in in the Jade Forest. After the Horde trained the hozen into an army, the jinyu became practice targets - a Grookin Soldier leads them below the hill to the Dookin' Grounds where Grookin Grenadiers test their bomb-throwing skills on the captives, exploding. Quotes Gossip Lucky for you I'm in this cage. My people and their new allies will burn this village to the ground. I destroyed twenty hozen before my capture! Hozen! Your time is short. We have new allies! Dialogue Patch changes External links Category:Jinyu Category:Prisoners Category:Jade Forest NPCs

Jinyu empire: The jinyu empire was the empire of the jinyu on Pandaria. In the years prior to Lei Shen's rise to power, the wise jinyu had arrived at the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and established a small empire. When the Thunder King began to see all other races within his domain as servants, he launched a brutal campaign against the jinyu. Though they fought valiantly, the fish-men eventually crumbled before the might of Lei Shen.World of Warcraft: Chronicle Volume 1, pg. 82 During one battle, the mogu fought against the jinyu empire in a battle that lasted forty days and forty nights. Haqin of the Hundred Spears countered their attacks and defenses and won the battle for the mogu. Afterwards, the Thunder King presented Haqin with a hundred of the finest golden spears crafted by mogu forgemasters. He became one of Lei Shen's greatest lieutenants, and after his death, the spears were buried at his side.Haqin of the Hundred Spears On the eve of the jinyu's final stand against the mogu, they were betrayed by their allies, the hozen, who had secretly made a pact with Lei Shen. The hozen believed that in exchange for their loyalty to the Thunder King, they would receive preferential treatment under the mogu empire's reign (a promise that was never kept). This betrayal ensured the jinyu's defeat and created a vicious feud between the two races that would last for centuries. Pearlfin Many thousand years ago, the Pearlfin Tribe stood on the throne of all Pandaria.

Where Are They Coming From?: If we do not discover where they're coming from and how they're able to work so quickly, we'll be overrun before we can act in force. Search to the southeast and see if you can find the source of these skeletons. Progress Have you found the source of the skeletons? Completion I suspected such. We can't let this continue. Our first priority is to destroy those summoning altars and then deal with the mess the Scourge has already created here. clear_direct.response:
# The Jinyu of World of Warcraft

The jinyu are a humanoid fish-like race native to Pandaria. They are an ancient and wise civilization, revered even by the pandaren themselves. As masters of water, they possess unique spiritual abilities—waterspeakers among them can literally hear what the land is saying by placing their staffs in rivers, allowing them to listen to the future.

The jinyu originated from murlocs who lived near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The magic of these sacred pools expanded their minds and grew their bodies, elevating them from simple creatures into one of Pandaria's great ancient cultures. They worship the August Celestials, who in turn offer them knowledge and guidance. The Temple of the Jade Serpent became particularly sacred to them, and they now use its scrying pool as their eyes and ears across the continent.

Before Lei Shen's rise to power, the jinyu established a thriving empire centered in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. However, the Thunder King viewed all other races as servants and launched a brutal campaign against them. During a legendary forty-day battle, the mogul general Haqin of the Hundred Spears defeated them decisively. The jinyu's final stand was sealed by betrayal—the hozen, their former allies, made a secret pact with Lei Shen, ensuring the jinyu's defeat and sparking a centuries-long feud between the two races.

# Der'izu Helm

## Where to Farm It

Der'izu Helm is a world drop from level 66-70 mobs. This means it can drop from any enemy in that level range, making it available through general grinding rather than a specific farm location.

## Possible Stats

The helm can roll with a variety of random enchantments. Here are the possible stat combinations:

- **+39 Stamina, +26 Intellect, +10 Mana Per 5 sec.**
- **+50 Stamina, +33 Spirit**
- **+33 Intellect, +33 Spirit**
- **+33 Intellect, +33 Agility**
- **+100 Attack Power**
- **+50 Stamina, +33 Frost/Arcane/Shadow/Fire/Nature Resistance**
- **+33 Intellect, +33 Strength**
- **+33 Spirit, +33 Agility**
- **+39 Stamina, +52 Attack Power, +26 Agility**
- **+26 Spell Critical Strike Rating, +30 Spell Damage and Healing, +26 Intellect**
- **+110 Healing**
- **+33 Intellect, +50 Stamina**
- **+75 Stamina**
- **+71 Nature Damage**
- **+50 Stamina, +33 Strength**
- **+39 Stamina, +26 Strength, +26 Critical Strike Rating**
- **+33 Strength, +33 Agility**
- **+50 Agility, +50 Strength, or +50 Intellect** (individual rolls)

The exact stats you receive will be randomized when the item drops.

clear_direct.response:
# Greater Cache of Treasures Rewards

Greater Caches of Treasures drop from random scenarios (after the first), subsequent heroic scenarios, and from two specific quests: "The King and the Council" and "The Warchief and the Darkness."

The cache contains the following rewards:

- **Gear**: Randomly enchanted items at item levels 450, 463, or 476
- **Currency**: 60 Justice Points
- **Materials**: 1-3x Mote of Harmony
- **Unique Items**: Vial of Reddish Ooze and The Bear and the Lady Fair (item)
- **Brawler's Guild items**: Various cosmetic or specialty items from the Brawler's Guild

The gear is spec-appropriate and randomly enchanted, making each cache a bit of a gamble in terms of exact stats.

match_tone.question: Who is Arthas Menethil?
match_tone.reference: Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and a Knight of the Silver Hand. He became a death knight after claiming the cursed runeblade Frostmourne in Northrend, sacrificing his soul in the process. He eventually became the Lich King after merging with Ner'zhul, and ruled from the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel until he was defeated by a group of adventurers.
snippets: Arthas Menethil: Arthas Menethil, Crown Prince of Lordaeron and Knight of the Silver Hand, was the son of King Terenas Menethil II and heir to the throne. He was trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and was inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand. Arthas also had a romantic relationship with the kind sorceress Jaina Proudmoore. Committed to the protection of his people, Arthas was determined to stop the plague spreading throughout Lordaeron. Despite his promising beginnings, Arthas became one of the most powerful and evil beings Azeroth would ever know. Although he killed the necromancer Kel'Thuzad, Arthas was unable to stop the spread of the plague and resorted to increasingly desperate methods, such as purging the city of Stratholme. Taking up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne, Arthas was able to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, but he lost his soul in the process. Becoming a death knight of the Scourge, he led the undead in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran. Arthas later made his way to the Frozen Throne of Icecrown and merged with the Lich King.March Badness Ruling as the Lich King for years afterward, Arthas had the last remnants of his humanity destroyed and was ultimately defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. Cradled by the spirit of his father, Arthas Menethil died, leaving the mantle of the Lich King to be taken by a noble soul who would contain the power of the Scourge.

match_tone.response:
# Arthas Menethil

Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and one of the most tragic figures in Warcraft history. Initially trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand, Arthas showed great promise as a protector of his people. He was romantically involved with Jaina Proudmoore and was determined to stop the plague spreading throughout his kingdom.

However, Arthas's descent into darkness was swift and catastrophic. Unable to contain the plague through conventional means, he resorted to increasingly desperate measures, including the purging of Stratholme. After wielding the cursed runeblade Frostmourne to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, Arthas lost his soul and became a death knight of the Scourge. He led the undead armies in the destruction of Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran before making his way to the Frozen Throne, where he merged with the Lich King himself.

Arthas ruled as the Lich King for years, his humanity completely destroyed in the process. He was ultimately defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and the champions of the Light. As he died, cradled by the spirit of his father King Terenas, the mantle of the Lich King passed to another soul capable of containing the Scourge's power. His fall stands as one of Azeroth's greatest tragedies—a noble prince consumed by desperation and darkness.

match_tone.question: What is Frostmourne?
match_tone.reference: Frostmourne is a cursed runeblade created by the Lich King. It was used by Arthas Menethil after he claimed it in Northrend, which caused him to lose his soul and become a death knight. The sword has the power to steal the souls of those it kills. It was eventually shattered during the final battle against the Lich King in Icecrown Citadel.
snippets: Frostmourne: thumb|upright=0.8|The Lich King holding Frostmourne. Frostmourne was the mourneblade of the Lich King. A fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword,Blood of the Highborne, chapter 2 it possessed the power to shatterA Paladin's Soul and imprison the souls of its victims and could remake the living into mindless undead. Frostmourne was thrust from the Frozen Throne by the Lich King Ner'zhul and was taken up by Arthas Menethil in the wastes of Northrend during the Third War. It was wielded by Arthas both during his time as a death knight and as Lich King until it was destroyed by Tirion Fordring; in that time, the blade spilled the blood of kings, destroyed nations, and inflicted scars on the world that remain to this day. Few weapons have shaped modern history as much as Frostmourne.Libram of the Dead: Blades of the Fallen Prince During the third invasion of the Burning Legion, a death knight hero reforged the shards of the blade into the Blades of the Fallen Prince. History Origin thumb|A vision of Frostmourne in the hands of the Jailer. Frostmourne and the Helm of Domination were crafted at the Runecarver's Oubliette in Torghast by the Runecarver, who was forced against his will to do so by the Jailer.

Just as the blade rends flesh, so must power scar the spirit". The ice-rimmed cavern that extends back into the mountains is full of saronite.Exploring Azeroth: Northrend, pg. 91 The cavern was given its name by the Scarlet Onslaught.Frostmourne Cavern (quest) At the time of the Third War, its entrance was protected by a group of revenants led by one of them known as The Guardian.Frostmourne (WC3 Human) When Arthas Menethil came with Muradin Bronzebeard to claim the artifact in his quest of destroying the dreadlord Mal'Ganis, the Guardian ordered them to turn back, and when they refused, a fight took place and all the revenants were destroyed. # Frostmourne

Frostmourne was the mourneblade of the Lich King—a fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword of immense power. The blade could shatter souls, imprison the souls of its victims within itself, and remake the living into mindless undead. Originally forged by the Runecarver at the Runecarver's Oubliette in Torghast under the Jailer's command, Frostmourne was thrust from the Frozen Throne and claimed by Arthas Menethil during the Third War in the frozen wastes of Northrend.

Arthas wielded the blade throughout his transformation into a death knight and his rise to become the Lich King himself. During his reign, Frostmourne spilled the blood of kings, destroyed entire nations, and left scars on the world that persist to this day. The weapon was ultimately destroyed by Tirion Fordring, though its shards were later reforged into the Blades of the Fallen Prince during the third invasion of the Burning Legion.

Few weapons in World of Warcraft history have shaped the course of events as profoundly as Frostmourne, which stands as one of the most iconic and consequential artifacts in the lore.

match_tone.question: What is the Wrathgate?
match_tone.reference: The Wrathgate, also known as Angrathar the Wrathgate, is a massive iron gate that serves as the entrance to Icecrown from the Dragonblight in Northrend. It was the site of a major battle where Horde and Alliance forces fought against the Scourge. The battle was interrupted when a faction of undead called the Forsaken Royal Apothecary Society unleashed a plague that killed both Scourge and Alliance and Horde forces, including High Overlord Saurfang the Younger.
snippets: Angrathar the Wrathgate: thumb|upright=1.1|Angrathar the Wrathgate. thumb|upright=1.1|After being cleaned by the red dragonflight. Angrathar the Wrathgate is a barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel and is located in the northwest of Dragonblight. It is a towering wall of knife edges made from saronite. During the war against the Lich King, the battle at the Wrath Gate was to be the first major blow against the Lich King, and the first in which the Alliance and Horde fought side by side instead of against each other. History Wrath of the Lich King thumb|Warlord Dranosh Saurfang and Highlord Bolvar Fordragon fighting the undead at the Wrathgate. During the war against the Lich King, the Wrath Gate was under siege by both the Alliance and the Horde forces, which set up the bases of Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard. Respectively led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang, their forces intended to defeat the Scourge forces at the gate and to storm into Icecrown Citadel. Their campaign culminated in the disastrous Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate, where they faced the Lich King himself, before being betrayed by Grand Apothecary Putress and his allies, who betrayed the Horde and all present by unleashing the New Plague on all the gathered forces. As the plague spread through the battlefield, many Horde and Alliance attempted to flee. The Lich King was also in serious danger of succumbing to the plague, and only barely managed to escape back through the Wrathgate.

# The Wrathgate

Angrathar the Wrathgate is a massive barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel located in northwestern Dragonblight. Constructed from towering knife-edged saronite, it served as the critical chokepoint in the assault against the Lich King during the Wrath of the Lich King expansion.

During the war against the Lich King, the Wrathgate became the site of an unprecedented military cooperation between the Alliance and Horde. Highlord Bolvar Fordragon led the Alliance forces from Fordragon Hold while Warlord Dranosh Saurfang commanded Horde troops from Kor'kron Vanguard, with both armies preparing to breach the gate and storm Icecrown Citadel itself.

However, the Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate became a catastrophic disaster. During the battle, Grand Apothecary Putress betrayed the combined forces by unleashing the New Plague across the battlefield, killing countless Alliance and Horde soldiers. The Lich King himself was forced to retreat through the Wrathgate to escape the plague, leaving both factions devastated and their offensive shattered.

match_tone.question: Who is Sylvanas Windrunner?
match_tone.reference: Sylvanas Windrunner is a former Ranger-General of Silvermoon who was killed by Arthas Menethil and raised as a banshee. She eventually regained her body and became the leader of the Forsaken undead faction. She later became Warchief of the Horde after Garrosh Hellscream and Vol'jin. She is known for burning Teldrassil and for her role in the Shadowlands storyline where she shattered the Helm of Domination.
snippets: Sylvanas Windrunner: Lady Sylvanas Windrunner, formerly styled "the Dark Lady" and "the Banshee Queen", is the former Warchief of the Horde and former supreme ruler of the Forsaken, one of the most powerful factions of undead on Azeroth. In life, Sylvanas was the ranger-general of Silvermoon, whose leadership acumen and martial prowess were without equal. During the Third War, she bravely defended Quel'Thalas from a Scourge invasion led by the death knight Arthas Menethil. Ultimately, however, Sylvanas fell in battle. Rather than honor the ranger-general with a quick death, Arthas ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee: a cunning and vengeful agent of the Lich King empowered by hate. When the Lich King's control over his minions weakened, Sylvanas broke away from her tyrannical master's control and reclaimed her body. Vowing to avenge her death, Sylvanas gathered other renegade undead and set out to wage war against the Scourge. Thus it was that the Forsaken came to be, with Sylvanas as their queen. Under Sylvanas' command, the Forsaken joined the Horde and later helped bring about the Lich King's fall in the frozen wastes of Northrend. She seeks a higher purpose for a people who have already died once.The Characters of Warcraft/Sylvanas Windrunner Yet many challenges still lay ahead for Sylvanas. After an uprising within her ranks that killed other members of the Horde, the banshee queen is now mistrusted by many of her allies.

Sylvanas Windrunner (tactics): Sylvanas Windrunner is the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination. The encounter is an epic battle that takes its start at the Pinnacle of Domination before continuing across the Edge of the Abyss into the Crucible in Oribos, all while Sylvanas tries to waylay the heroes of Azeroth and prevent them from interfering with the Jailer's plan. Adventure Guide With Oribos in peril, a desperate battle at the pinnacle of the Sanctum begins. Standing between the Jailer and the mortals who would stop him is Sylvanas Windrunner. With no masks left to wear, the Banshee Queen unleashes her full power to protect the Banished One while his ultimate plan unfolds. Overview Azeroth's champions battle Sylvanas at the pinnacle of the Sanctum of Domination, across the vastness between the Maw and the Eternal City, and within the Arbiter's chamber. Players first encounter Sylvanas atop Torghast. Upon reaching 85% health, she rips apart chunks of the tower itself. As the Jailer envelops Oribos in the Maw, players pursue him with the aid of Thrall, Jaina Proudmoore, and Bolvar Fordragon, forging new paths and defeating Mawforged commanders while Sylvanas harrows from all sides in Banshee Form. Within the Crucible, the Banished One executes his grand plan. Our allies confront the dominated Anduin Wrynn while Sylvanas engages players directly. Damage Dealers – While under the effects of Windrunner, spells and abilities strike Sylvanas where she cast Windrunner.

match_tone.response:
# Sylvanas Windrunner

Sylvanas Windrunner is one of World of Warcraft's most pivotal figures. In life, she was the ranger-general of Silvermoon and defended Quel'Thalas with unmatched leadership and martial prowess. When Arthas invaded during the Third War, he killed her in battle and transformed her soul into a banshee—a powerful undead agent bound to his will by the Lich King's control.

Breaking free from the Lich King's domination, Sylvanas reclaimed her body and swore vengeance against the Scourge. She gathered other renegade undead and founded the Forsaken, becoming their queen. The Forsaken eventually joined the Horde, and under Sylvanas' command, they played a crucial role in defeating the Lich King in Northrend. She served as Warchief of the Horde and supreme ruler of the Forsaken, seeking a higher purpose for her undead people.

In the Shadowlands, Sylvanas served as the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination, fighting heroes across multiple realms to protect the Jailer's plan. Her character arc represents a tragic fall from honored ranger-general to a complex figure driven by trauma, vengeance, and an obsession with finding meaning in undeath. Once styled the "Dark Lady" and "Banshee Queen," Sylvanas remains one of the most formidable and controversial figures in Azeroth's history.

match_tone.question: What is the Lich King?
match_tone.reference: The Lich King is the ruler of the undead Scourge and resides on the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel in Northrend. The original Lich King was Ner'zhul, an orc shaman whose spirit was trapped in armor by Kil'jaeden. Arthas Menethil later merged with Ner'zhul to become the new Lich King. After Arthas was defeated, Bolvar Fordragon became the new Lich King to keep the Scourge in check.
snippets: Lich King: While the Lich King dreamt, the various personas in his mind - the death knight Arthas Menethil, the orc shaman Ner'zhul, and Matthias Lehner, the personification of the remnants of Arthas's humanity - fought for influence and control over the entity. Matthias tried to reason with Arthas, but Arthas silenced him by running him through with Frostmourne. Ner'zhul was delighted by this, declaring that he and Arthas were now free to merge into a single glorious being. Arthas rejected this offer, stating that once he had the power of the Lich King, no one would tell him what to do again. He impaled the stunned Ner'zhul with Frostmourne, becoming the dominant personality of the Lich King and ending the dream.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, Epilogue In his quiet meditation, the part of the Lich King that was Arthas began to reflect on the events of his life, on the path that had taken him so far from the young boy who who had been his father's pride and joy. In the midst of his reflections, Arthas sensed a bitter and ancient presence locked within the nearby ice, and with this realization came the name Sindragosa.The Cinematic Art of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King Wrath of the Lich King Awakening thumb|upright=1.2|The awakened Lich King and his undead armies in Icecrown.

Lich King (tactics): The Lich King is the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and the final major encounter of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King. This boss has been available since February 2, 2010 on US servers and February 3 on EU servers. The Lich King is a long and challenging battle for survival. All classes are tested to the limits of their abilities, in particular all raid members have to react quickly to various abilities, often several at the same time. The encounter is rather unforgiving; most players must react properly in various situations, and all players get ample opportunity to commit mistakes which then wipe the raid. This encounter is a fitting end to Wrath of the Lich King and on par with Yogg-Saron in Ulduar. Abilities The Lich King melees for roughly 40,000 on an item level 264 geared tank on 25-player difficulty. The Lich King enrages after 15 minutes of combat, gaining 900% damage and 150% attack speed. However, he is notably still affected by taunt abilities. Phase 1 Phase 1.5 and 2.5 Phase 2 The Lich King retains Infest. Phase 3 The Lich King retains Soul Reaper and Defile. Frostmourne Spirit Warden Terenas Menethil Phase 4 Terenas Menethil Preparation The fight requires the standard raid composition of 2/3 Tanks and 3/5 healers (10/25 player), while learning the encounter more healers can help. No resistance or other special gear is required.

Icon of the Lich King: thumb|250px|Icon of the Lich King. The Icon of the Lich King is the sigil of the Scourge. Its azure coloration and icy ornamentation symbolize the frigid homeland of the undead horde in Northrend. At the icon's center is the now-shattered runeblade Frostmourne, the former blade of the Lich King Arthas Menethil. The frost-covered hammers flanking the blade represent the Scourge's endless capacity for warfare, as well as their construction of the saronite citadel Icecrown and the dread Necropoli that shepherded the endless armies of the dead and served as hubs of unspeakable torment upon the living. Although the holder of the Helm of Domination has changed since the creation of the sigil, and the goals of the Scourge along with them, this sigil seems to have remained representative of the organization. The Cult of the Damned, an affiliate group consisting primarily of the living, retains both a unique icon and the twisted ambitions of the original Scourge. Gallery See also Icon Category:Icons

# The Lich King

The Lich King is the supreme ruler of the Scourge, an undead horde that commands endless armies of the dead from Northrend. The entity represents a fusion of multiple personalities: the death knight Arthas Menethil, the orc shaman Ner'zhul, and Matthias Lehner (the remnants of Arthas's humanity). During a internal struggle for dominance, Arthas eliminated both rivals, becoming the dominant personality and absolute master of the Lich King's power.

The Lich King serves as the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and the climactic encounter of the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The battle tests raid members to their limits with complex mechanics across multiple phases, enraging after 15 minutes and becoming increasingly lethal. The entity wields the runeblade Frostmourne and commands abilities like Soul Reaper and Defile to devastate opponents.

The Icon of the Lich King—featuring the shattered Frostmourne blade and frost-covered hammers—symbolizes the Scourge's dominion. Its azure coloration represents Northrend's frozen wastelands, while the imagery reflects both the endless capacity for warfare and the construction of Icecrown Citadel and the Necropoli that channeled the dead into eternal torment.

match_tone.question: What is Illidan Stormrage known for?
match_tone.reference: Illidan Stormrage is a night elf demon hunter and the twin brother of Malfurion Stormrage. He is known for drinking from the Skull of Gul'dan which transformed him into a demon-like being. He was imprisoned for ten thousand years for creating a new Well of Eternity. After being freed he took control of Outland and the Black Temple. He is often associated with the quote 'You are not prepared.' He was later revealed to be a crucial weapon against the Burning Legion.
snippets: Illidan Stormrage: While Malfurion had spent a lifetime fighting for the dream of what Azeroth could be, he now must fight for what it is. With his final words, Illidan asked Malfurion to take care of Tyrande and listen to her counsel, for she was always the best of them. The road ahead would be long, but whatever came, he knew Malfurion would bring honor to the name Stormrage. To Tyrande, he spoke of how all the paths he had taken had led to a single purpose, saving the world; he could bide no half measures or compromises. He told her how whenever he faced doubt, he held on to one anchor: Her. He told Tyrande that she embodied the best of Azeroth with her faith and devotion, and asked her to take care of Malfurion. While he sometimes wished her heart had made a different choice, in the end he knew she made the right one. Atop Hyjal, on the shores of the Well of Eternity that he created ages ago, Illidan told the adventurer that they had proven their commitment to Azeroth, that their dedication and sacrifice rivaled his own.

The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage: The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage is the continuation of the Illidan Stormrage campaign in Patch 7.1.0. Objectives Seek out Allari the Souleater at Illidari Stand in Azsuna. Description Allari the Souleater, an oracle of the Illidari, has been on the hunt for Illidan's soul for quite some time. She now believes that she has discovered its location and requires your assistance in its recovery. Find Allari at Illidari Stand in Azsuna and aid her in any way possible. If Sargeras truly plans to use Illidan's body as a vessel for his own return then returning Illidan's soul to his body is integral in preventing the dark titan from returning to our world! Rewards You will receive: Completion The naaru heard my call? Interesting... Very well, let's get to work. We've found Illidan's soul - finally - and it's not where we expected it to be held. Progression Patch changes External links

Illidan Stormrage (tactics): Illidan Stormrage is the final boss of the Black Temple. The fight consists of five phases, and good positioning is the key to success. Biography Adventure Guide Overview Illidan wields his Warglaives at the start of the encounter, infecting foes with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Upon reaching 65% health, Illidan takes flight and throws the Warglaives of Azzinoth to the ground, each of which unleashes a Flame. The two Flames of Azzinoth must be defeated to bring Illidan to the ground. For the remainder of the encounter, Illidan periodically unleashes the power of the demonic force within him, using those dark powers to assault his foes, before returning to his natural form. At 30% health, Maiev arrives to assist in handling the Betrayer's final efforts to overpower his foes. file:Dps_icon.png Damage Dealers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Healer_icon.png Healers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Tank_icon.png Tanks Use Active Mitigation defensive abilities to survive Shear. Move Illidan Stormrage to avoid damage taken from Flame Crash. In Stage Two, be quick to grab the attention of the Flames of Azzinoth.

Lord Illidan Stormrage (quest): Lord Illidan Stormrage is the second of the three storyline quests, available starting at reputation with Netherwing. Objectives Meet Illidan Stormrage. Seek out Barthamus in the Lower City of Shattrath should you survive. You will need: Meeting with Illidan Stormrage Description Come, highlord . Illidan himself awaits us! Completion I was the first, you know. The abandoned child of a monster... We owe you everything, . You have single-handedly shattered the Dragonmaw empire and gathered enough information to keep my brethren occupied for ten lifetimes. It is not much but there is something that we can offer... My kin have each offered to join you on your mission in Outland. Simply ask and they will bond with you. Quest Dialogue During this quest, Overlord Mor'ghor summons an image of Illidan at the Dragonmaw Base Camp to meet the "Dragonmaw orc" (the player in disguise) who will be their champion: An image of Illidan appears. Illidan immobilizes you. Illidan's image vanishes. Yarzill the Merc transforms into a nether dragon! Notes thumb|Riding off into the sunset on Yarzill's back At the end of the dialogue sequence, Yarzill transforms into a nether dragon and carries the player to Shattrath City. The trip is slow, as Yarzill flies at about walking speed. It is advised to have the necessary time before triggering this quest, although as of patch 2.3.0 the speed has been raised.

Awakenings: Objectives Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage in Val'sharah. Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage Description Light's Heart is to serve as the vessel for Illidan Stormrage's rebirth: my last act of service to the Light. But Illidan is dead and his soul is lost in the Nether. Before we may attempt to recover his soul, we must prepare the vessel for his return. Across two worlds exist echoes of Illidan's past. You must venture out and find the echoes. Start where all life begins, : birth. Find Illidan's birthplace in Val'sharah. Awaken the memory. Rewards You will receive: Progress When our work is complete, I will be lost to the Great Dark. Completion An auspicious start to a life of tragedy. Perhaps one of the few joyous memories Illidan would have in his life. While the years that followed tested the prophesied child, they would never break him. You must remember that, despite what you may see as our journey continues. There is much more to be done, but I am not yet ready. I will call for you when it is time. Gains 20,560 XP Notes Go to Lorlathil in Val'sharah. This will prompt a message from Xe'ra. Press the extra action button to see the cinematic. thumb|Elves in front of the newborn Stormrage twins. A crowd of night elves has assembled in Lorlathil around two cribs, presided by a lead Priestess of the Moon wearing the Raiment of Divine Clarity set, and six more priestesses each wearing a Mooncloth Robe.

# Illidan Stormrage

Illidan Stormrage is one of World of Warcraft's most complex and pivotal characters. He's a night elf demon hunter who has served as both antagonist and unlikely hero throughout Azeroth's history. His defining characteristic is his unwavering commitment to saving the world—a goal he pursued through any means necessary, refusing to accept half measures or compromises.

As a powerful warrior, Illidan wields his iconic Warglaives of Azzinoth and commands demonic forces, making him a formidable opponent. He served as the final boss of the Black Temple, one of World of Warcraft's most legendary raids. Beyond combat prowess, Illidan was deeply connected to the night elves' greatest figures—he was the twin brother of Malfurion Stormrage and harbored profound feelings for Tyrande Whisperwind, though he accepted her choice to be with Malfurion.

Illidan's most enduring legacy is his paradoxical nature: despite his demonic appearance and ruthless methods, his actions ultimately served to protect Azeroth from catastrophic threats. In his final moments, he entrusted the world's continued defense to those he'd fought alongside, cementing his transformation from the "Betrayer" to a hero willing to sacrifice everything for the world he fought to save.

# Mystics in Warcraft III

Mystics were night elf units that appeared exclusively in the Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos mission "Brothers in Blood." They served as part of the Barrow Deeps Watchers, stationed in a prison's library where they guarded magical artifacts. Unlike most night elf units in the game, Mystics were male characters with a distinctive appearance—they resembled human Apprentice Wizards tinted red, making them visually unique among night elf forces.

Combat-wise, Mystics were spellcasters that cast shamanistic spells rather than the typical druid magic associated with night elves. Their primary ability was **Purge**, which removed buffs from target units, slowed their movement speed significantly, and dealt substantial damage to summoned units. While they technically had access to the Shaman version of Bloodlust, this ability was never researched and therefore unavailable in actual gameplay.

In Warcraft III: Reforged, the Mystics' appearance was updated to resemble high elves instead of their original design. Lore-wise, they share similarities with night elven lorekeepers—both groups are hidden away in underground places of knowledge, suggesting they served as guardians of magical secrets and artifacts rather than frontline warriors.

Banshee (Warcraft III): Banshees were once beautiful female Night Elves who were brutally murdered by demons during the fall of Kalimdor. Their restless spirits were left to wander the world for many ages in silent, tortured lamentation. Over the years they became jealous of the living and seeded a deep hatred for all life. Yet when the Lich King, Ner'zhul, became aware of them, he gathered them together and promised them vengeance upon the living. Ner'zhul gave them terrible voices so that the living would finally hear their timeless anguish. Thus, the Banshees have pledged themselves to the Undead cause and serve as valuable warriors in the Scourge. The spectral Banshees can fly and are fond of hammering their enemies with tortured blasts of sonic force. Their unnerving, unholy screams can be heard from miles away. Information Banshees are very good at supporting units, coming with their initial spell of Curse and can later learn Anti-Magic Shell and Possession. They're still fairly weak like Necromancers and need protection. Banshees are the bane of any army that is heavily invested into ground units, as they can steal them and turn them against their owner if given the chance. # Sylvanas Windrunner (Banshee Form) in Warcraft III

## Obtaining Her

In the original Warcraft III, you receive Sylvanas as a named banshee by completing an optional quest upon Silvermoon's fall in "The Fall of Silvermoon" campaign mission. In Warcraft III: Reforged, the process is simplified—you only need to reach the gates of Silvermoon to defeat her and obtain her unit.

## Abilities

Sylvanas in banshee form has two primary abilities:

- **Curse (Autocast)** - Curses a target enemy unit, giving it a 33% chance to miss on attacks
- **Anti-Magic Shell** - Creates a barrier that grants spell immunity to a target unit for 90 seconds (costs 50 mana, 50 range)

In Reforged, her banshee unit is technically represented by her Dark Ranger form with banshee aspects, featuring modifications like Howl of Terror instead of Black Arrow, though the core gameplay remains similar to the classic banshee unit.

# The Original Harvester of Wrath

The original Harvester of Wrath was one of the first venthyr Harvesters created directly by Sire Denathrius. However, her power was stripped from her long ago and given to the Stonewright, who has held the title of Harvester of Wrath ever since and is now referred to as the second Harvester of that title.

The original Harvester still exists somewhere, but her whereabouts are unknown. According to the Curator, she hasn't been seen for "a few epochs." The confusion surrounding her successor arose when Prince Renathal mistakenly described the Stonewright as one of the first venthyr, until the Curator corrected him and clarified that he was confusing the Stonewright with her predecessor.

match_tone.question: Who is Lei Shen and what did he do in World of Warcraft?
match_tone.reference: Lei Shen, also known as the Thunder King, was the first emperor of the mogu and the most powerful warlord in mogu history. After stealing the power of storms from the titan keeper Ra, he united the mogu clans into the first great mogu empire and made significant contributions to Pandaria, most notably constructing the Serpent's Spine barrier to separate the mantid from the rest of the Empire. In recent times, he was resurrected by Zandalari allies and attempted to unify the mogu again, but was defeated by a combined assault from the Shado-Pan, Alliance, and Horde on the Isle of Thunder.
snippets: Lei Shen: It is said that this early rivalry was instrumental in developing Lei Shen's thirst for power.Sparkmancer Vu epitaph Despite seeing the mogu's infighting and squabbling as a betrayal of their great potential, Lei Shen remained a stalwart vassal of his father. A close advisor eventually betrayed and murdered Lei Shen's father, causing the clan to fall apart and seek refuge with other warlords, leaving Lei Shen and a handful of loyal guardsmen behind. Rather than continue the fighting, the young mogu chose to go into exile, meditating and wandering the land. Most mogu saw Lei Shen's meditations as a sign of madness and proof that he would never threaten any mogu ever again. The power of Ra-den Like his brothers, Lei Shen was strong of arm and fluent in the ways of war. But unlike others of his generation, he spoke of more than conquest. The legends of his forefathers were etched into his heart. Once Lei Shen had come of age and commanded his own legions, he announced his intent to awaken the gods. After years of searching, the warlord found the entrance to the hidden vaults beneath the lands north of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. What he found therein was illuminating: the titanic watcher Ra, who stood guard over a powerful titan device, the Engine of Nalak'sha.The Characters of Warcraft/Lei Shen The Highkeeper showed no reaction upon seeing Lei Shen, even as the mogu began asking him questions about the mogu's purpose.

Lei Shen (tactics): Lei Shen is the twelfth and final boss encounter in the Throne of Thunder on Looking for Raid and Normal Mode. Background Adventure Guide Tyrant. Dictator. Conqueror. Emperor. These titles of the Thunder King were earned by bringing absolute misery to every race of Pandaria. Resurrected into a world where his people no longer rule, Lei Shen is bent on ending the farce and bringing the land under his heel once more. Encounter design The core concept for the Lei Shen fight developed during a brainstorming session as members of the encounter design team were throwing around ideas for potential fight mechanics, trying to see if anything would stick. A popular idea was for him to need to pause and recharge periodically, but someone suggested an interesting twist on that idea—the Thunder King isn't just a battery that needs to recharge; he's actually a power source himself. So instead of him drawing power from his citadel, the citadel draws power from him. He is a mighty warrior in his own right, with distinctive weapon and lightning attacks, but his mere presence energizes and activates portions of his citadel’s defenses, and managing those becomes a central element of the encounter.

Foot of Lei Shen: thumb|Foot of Lei Shen. The Foot of Lei Shen is the last step before reaching the Throne of Thunder on the northern part of the Isle of Thunder. The area is unlocked after completing The Fall of Shan Bu, which finishes The Fall of Shan Bu stage of the Isle of Thunder storyline.
