# Finding Quests and Trainers in Dun Morogh

Dun Morogh is the starting zone for dwarves and gnomes, with Coldridge Valley serving as the sheltered leveling area for levels 1-6. New players will find the opening quests straightforward and can begin by exploring the zone or consulting the in-game hints if needed.

The zone contains numerous questgivers and class trainers scattered throughout. For warriors specifically, Thran Khorman is located in Anvilmar and offers quests like "Simple Rune" and "Getting Battle-Ready." Beyond the starting area, you'll find the major dwarven city of Ironforge, which serves as one of the Alliance's primary trade and travel hubs and houses additional trainers and services. The zone also features professional trainers for tradeskills if you want to develop those while leveling.

For a complete list of all available quests organized by level, there's a dedicated Dun Morogh quests table. If you're seeking the most efficient quest completion order rather than power-leveling, a questing guide is available that shows the optimal path through all available quests in the zone.

# Kelmarin: A Ranger of Distinction

Kelmarin was a high elf ranger who served under Sylvanas Windrunner's command during the Third War. He earned distinction among his peers as one of three scouts tasked with investigating the approaching Scourge army, alongside his fellow rangers Sheldaris and Vor'athil. Sylvanas held him in particularly high regard, knowing his information was always accurate and detailed.

While Sheldaris and Vor'athil waited for Sylvanas to arrive with the main force of rangers, Kelmarin took the initiative to return and report directly to Sylvanas about the advancing Scourge threat. His scouting efforts and timely intelligence proved crucial during this critical period of the war. Kelmarin's loyalty and competence made him a trusted advisor to Sylvanas during one of the most significant conflicts in Warcraft history, though his ultimate fate following the Third War remains unrecorded in the available lore.

Jinyu empire: The jinyu empire was the empire of the jinyu on Pandaria. In the years prior to Lei Shen's rise to power, the wise jinyu had arrived at the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and established a small empire. When the Thunder King began to see all other races within his domain as servants, he launched a brutal campaign against the jinyu. Though they fought valiantly, the fish-men eventually crumbled before the might of Lei Shen.World of Warcraft: Chronicle Volume 1, pg. 82 During one battle, the mogu fought against the jinyu empire in a battle that lasted forty days and forty nights. Haqin of the Hundred Spears countered their attacks and defenses and won the battle for the mogu. Afterwards, the Thunder King presented Haqin with a hundred of the finest golden spears crafted by mogu forgemasters. He became one of Lei Shen's greatest lieutenants, and after his death, the spears were buried at his side.Haqin of the Hundred Spears On the eve of the jinyu's final stand against the mogu, they were betrayed by their allies, the hozen, who had secretly made a pact with Lei Shen. The hozen believed that in exchange for their loyalty to the Thunder King, they would receive preferential treatment under the mogu empire's reign (a promise that was never kept). This betrayal ensured the jinyu's defeat and created a vicious feud between the two races that would last for centuries. Pearlfin Many thousand years ago, the Pearlfin Tribe stood on the throne of all Pandaria.

match_tone.response:
# The Jinyu of World of Warcraft

The jinyu are a race of humanoid fish-like creatures native to Pandaria. They evolved from murlocs who lived near the enchanted pools of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms—the magic of these ancient pools expanded their minds and grew their bodies, transforming them into one of Pandaria's great ancient cultures. They worshipped the August Celestials in exchange for knowledge and guidance, developing into an elder and wise race respected even by the pandaren themselves.

The jinyu are masters of the water with profound spiritual abilities. Their waterspeakers—an upper caste of spiritual leaders—use rivers to listen to the future and commune with the land itself. They held the Temple of the Jade Serpent as sacred, using its scrying pool as their eyes and ears across the continent. However, their civilization fell to ruin when Lei Shen, the Thunder King, launched a brutal campaign against them. On the eve of their final stand, the jinyu were betrayed by their hozen allies, who had made a secret pact with Lei Shen, leading to the fish-men's eventual defeat and centuries of enmity between the two races.

Some jinyu eventually left Pandaria to hunt in the ocean depths, becoming the ankoan—a distinct but related people who maintain their connection to their jinyu ancestry.

match_tone.response:
# Der'izu Helm Location and Stats

The Der'izu Helm is a **world drop** that can be looted from mobs between levels 66-70, so you can farm it from any humanoid enemies in that level range across Outland zones.

The helm has extensive random enchantment options, allowing it to roll with various stat combinations depending on the suffix. Some notable possibilities include stamina-focused rolls (up to +75 Stamina), intellect builds (+50 Intellect), strength variants (+50 Strength), or specialized suffixes like healing (+110 Healing), spell damage (+30 Spell Damage and Healing), and various resistance options (Frost, Fire, Shadow, Arcane, and Nature Protection all grant +50 Stamina and +33 Resistance).

If you're looking for a specific appearance, the Der'izu Helm is also part of the **Ensemble: Der'izu Armor** set, which can be purchased from transmogrifiers during the Trial of Style event and will unlock the appearance for transmog.

match_tone.response:
# Greater Cache of Treasures Rewards

Greater Caches of Treasures drop from randomly-selected scenarios, heroic scenarios (after the first), and specific quests like "The King and the Council" and "The Warchief and the Darkness."

The cache contains a variety of rewards including Brawler's Guild items, the Vial of Reddish Ooze, the item "The Bear and the Lady Fair," and 1-3 Motes of Harmony. You'll also receive 60 Justice Points and a random enchanted item ranging from item level 450 to 476 depending on the difficulty of the scenario you completed.

no_adjectives.question: Who is Arthas Menethil?
no_adjectives.reference: Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and a Knight of the Silver Hand. He became a death knight after claiming the cursed runeblade Frostmourne in Northrend, sacrificing his soul in the process. He eventually became the Lich King after merging with Ner'zhul, and ruled from the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel until he was defeated by a group of adventurers.
snippets: Arthas Menethil: Arthas Menethil, Crown Prince of Lordaeron and Knight of the Silver Hand, was the son of King Terenas Menethil II and heir to the throne. He was trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and was inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand. Arthas also had a romantic relationship with the kind sorceress Jaina Proudmoore. Committed to the protection of his people, Arthas was determined to stop the plague spreading throughout Lordaeron. Despite his promising beginnings, Arthas became one of the most powerful and evil beings Azeroth would ever know. Although he killed the necromancer Kel'Thuzad, Arthas was unable to stop the spread of the plague and resorted to increasingly desperate methods, such as purging the city of Stratholme. Taking up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne, Arthas was able to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, but he lost his soul in the process. Becoming a death knight of the Scourge, he led the undead in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran. Arthas later made his way to the Frozen Throne of Icecrown and merged with the Lich King.March Badness Ruling as the Lich King for years afterward, Arthas had the last remnants of his humanity destroyed and was ultimately defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. Cradled by the spirit of his father, Arthas Menethil died, leaving the mantle of the Lich King to be taken by a noble soul who would contain the power of the Scourge.

no_adjectives.response:
# Arthas Menethil

Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and one of the most significant figures in World of Warcraft lore. He began as a noble paladin trained by Uther the Lightbringer and inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand, with a romantic relationship with the sorceress Jaina Proudmoore. However, he became one of the most powerful and evil beings Azeroth has ever known.

When a devastating plague spread throughout Lordaeron, Arthas grew increasingly desperate to stop it. He resorted to brutal methods, including the purging of Stratholme. Taking up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne, he defeated the demon Mal'Ganis but lost his soul in the process, becoming a death knight of the Scourge. He then led the undead in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran, ultimately merging with the Lich King at the Frozen Throne of Icecrown.

Arthas ruled as the Lich King for years, with his remaining humanity stripped away until he was defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and champions of the Light. He died cradled by the spirit of his father, King Terenas Menethil II, passing the mantle of the Lich King to another who could contain the Scourge's power.

It was used by Arthas Menethil after he claimed it in Northrend, which caused him to lose his soul and become a death knight. The sword has the power to steal the souls of those it kills. It was eventually shattered during the final battle against the Lich King in Icecrown Citadel.

A fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword,Blood of the Highborne, chapter 2 it possessed the power to shatterA Paladin's Soul and imprison the souls of its victims and could remake the living into mindless undead. Frostmourne was thrust from the Frozen Throne by the Lich King Ner'zhul and was taken up by Arthas Menethil in the wastes of Northrend during the Third War. It was wielded by Arthas both during his time as a death knight and as Lich King until it was destroyed by Tirion Fordring; in that time, the blade spilled the blood of kings, destroyed nations, and inflicted scars on the world that remain to this day. Few weapons have shaped modern history as much as Frostmourne.Libram of the Dead: Blades of the Fallen Prince During the third invasion of the Burning Legion, a death knight hero reforged the shards of the blade into the Blades of the Fallen Prince. History Origin thumb|A vision of Frostmourne in the hands of the Jailer. In its final moments, the Guardian attempted to stop Arthas, explaining that he was not protecting the sword, but everyone from it. no_adjectives.response:
# Frostmourne

Frostmourne is the legendary runeblade of the Lich King—a fearsome, rune-inscribed two-handed longsword that stands as one of the most pivotal weapons in World of Warcraft's history. Crafted by the Runecarver at Torghast under compulsion by the Jailer, Frostmourne possessed devastating power: it could shatter souls, imprison the spirits of its victims within the blade itself, and corrupt the living into mindless undead servants.

The blade was claimed by Arthas Menethil during the Third War when he discovered it resting on an altar in Frostmourne Cavern in Northrend. Arthas wielded it throughout his transformation into a death knight and later as the Lich King himself, using it to conquer nations and inflict lasting scars upon the world. The sword's influence shaped the course of modern history more than few other weapons. It was ultimately destroyed by Tirion Fordring during the confrontation at the Frozen Throne, though the weapon's legacy continued when a death knight hero reforged its shards into the Blades of the Fallen Prince during the third invasion of the Burning Legion.

no_adjectives.question: What is the Wrathgate?
no_adjectives.reference: The Wrathgate, also known as Angrathar the Wrathgate, is a massive iron gate that serves as the entrance to Icecrown from the Dragonblight in Northrend. It was the site of a major battle where Horde and Alliance forces fought against the Scourge. The battle was interrupted when a faction of undead called the Forsaken Royal Apothecary Society unleashed a plague that killed both Scourge and Alliance and Horde forces, including High Overlord Saurfang the Younger.
snippets: Angrathar the Wrathgate: thumb|upright=1.1|Angrathar the Wrathgate. thumb|upright=1.1|After being cleaned by the red dragonflight. Angrathar the Wrathgate is a barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel and is located in the northwest of Dragonblight. It is a towering wall of knife edges made from saronite. During the war against the Lich King, the battle at the Wrath Gate was to be the first major blow against the Lich King, and the first in which the Alliance and Horde fought side by side instead of against each other. History Wrath of the Lich King thumb|Warlord Dranosh Saurfang and Highlord Bolvar Fordragon fighting the undead at the Wrathgate. During the war against the Lich King, the Wrath Gate was under siege by both the Alliance and the Horde forces, which set up the bases of Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard. Respectively led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang, their forces intended to defeat the Scourge forces at the gate and to storm into Icecrown Citadel. Their campaign culminated in the disastrous Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate, where they faced the Lich King himself, before being betrayed by Grand Apothecary Putress and his allies, who betrayed the Horde and all present by unleashing the New Plague on all the gathered forces. As the plague spread through the battlefield, many Horde and Alliance attempted to flee. The Lich King was also in serious danger of succumbing to the plague, and only barely managed to escape back through the Wrathgate.

no_adjectives.response:
# The Wrathgate

The Wrathgate, officially known as Angrathar the Wrathgate, is a towering barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel located in the northwest of Dragonblight. It is constructed from saronite and features a distinctive design of knife-like edges. This monumental structure served as the primary gateway to the Lich King's fortress during the war in Northrend.

The Wrathgate became historically significant during the Wrath of the Lich King expansion when the Alliance and Horde launched their first coordinated assault against the Lich King. Led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang respectively, the two factions established bases—Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard—to besiege the gate and breach Icecrown Citadel. However, this campaign culminated in the catastrophic Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate, where Grand Apothecary Putress betrayed both factions by unleashing the New Plague on all gathered forces. The plague devastated the battlefield, forcing both Alliance and Horde soldiers to flee and nearly killing the Lich King himself before he escaped back through the gate.

no_adjectives.question: Who is Sylvanas Windrunner?
no_adjectives.reference: Sylvanas Windrunner is a former Ranger-General of Silvermoon who was killed by Arthas Menethil and raised as a banshee. She eventually regained her body and became the leader of the Forsaken undead faction. She later became Warchief of the Horde after Garrosh Hellscream and Vol'jin. She is known for burning Teldrassil and for her role in the Shadowlands storyline where she shattered the Helm of Domination.
snippets: Sylvanas Windrunner: Lady Sylvanas Windrunner, formerly styled "the Dark Lady" and "the Banshee Queen", is the former Warchief of the Horde and former supreme ruler of the Forsaken, one of the most powerful factions of undead on Azeroth. In life, Sylvanas was the ranger-general of Silvermoon, whose leadership acumen and martial prowess were without equal. During the Third War, she bravely defended Quel'Thalas from a Scourge invasion led by the death knight Arthas Menethil. Ultimately, however, Sylvanas fell in battle. Rather than honor the ranger-general with a quick death, Arthas ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee: a cunning and vengeful agent of the Lich King empowered by hate. When the Lich King's control over his minions weakened, Sylvanas broke away from her tyrannical master's control and reclaimed her body. Vowing to avenge her death, Sylvanas gathered other renegade undead and set out to wage war against the Scourge. Thus it was that the Forsaken came to be, with Sylvanas as their queen. Under Sylvanas' command, the Forsaken joined the Horde and later helped bring about the Lich King's fall in the frozen wastes of Northrend. She seeks a higher purpose for a people who have already died once.The Characters of Warcraft/Sylvanas Windrunner Yet many challenges still lay ahead for Sylvanas. After an uprising within her ranks that killed other members of the Horde, the banshee queen is now mistrusted by many of her allies.

Sylvanas Windrunner (tactics): Sylvanas Windrunner is the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination. The encounter is an epic battle that takes its start at the Pinnacle of Domination before continuing across the Edge of the Abyss into the Crucible in Oribos, all while Sylvanas tries to waylay the heroes of Azeroth and prevent them from interfering with the Jailer's plan. Adventure Guide With Oribos in peril, a desperate battle at the pinnacle of the Sanctum begins. Standing between the Jailer and the mortals who would stop him is Sylvanas Windrunner. With no masks left to wear, the Banshee Queen unleashes her full power to protect the Banished One while his ultimate plan unfolds. Overview Azeroth's champions battle Sylvanas at the pinnacle of the Sanctum of Domination, across the vastness between the Maw and the Eternal City, and within the Arbiter's chamber. Players first encounter Sylvanas atop Torghast. Upon reaching 85% health, she rips apart chunks of the tower itself. As the Jailer envelops Oribos in the Maw, players pursue him with the aid of Thrall, Jaina Proudmoore, and Bolvar Fordragon, forging new paths and defeating Mawforged commanders while Sylvanas harrows from all sides in Banshee Form. Within the Crucible, the Banished One executes his grand plan. Our allies confront the dominated Anduin Wrynn while Sylvanas engages players directly. Damage Dealers – While under the effects of Windrunner, spells and abilities strike Sylvanas where she cast Windrunner.

Windrunner: The Windrunners are a prominent high elf and half-elf family. Their residence was a spire in a village both named after them in what is now known as the Ghostlands. Family tree History thumb|left|The Windrunner sisters dropping their necklaces into the sea at the end of their failed reunion The Windrunner family has a tragic story. Several members of the family were thought to be killed by the orcs during the Second War, including the sisters' parents, their youngest brother Lirath, along with numerous relatives of varying proximity.Warcraft II: Beyond the Dark Portal manual, Legends of the Land, AlleriaBeyond the Dark Portal, pg. 68 Lireesa as the ranger-general of Silvermoon died during the attack, and Sylvanas became the new ranger-general, but gave :Thas'dorah, Legacy of the Windrunners to her older sister, Alleria, who was a distinguished Farstrider. Alleria would wield Thas'dorah in battle against the Legion for centuries before it was lost on Niskara and eventually retrieved by a hunter and her younger sister Vereesa.Tales of the Hunt Only Alleria, Sylvanas, Vereesa, and Zendarin are known to have survived.

# Sylvanas Windrunner

Sylvanas Windrunner is one of World of Warcraft's most pivotal characters. Originally the Ranger-General of Silvermoon, she was a skilled military leader whose martial prowess was unmatched. During the Third War, she heroically defended Quel'Thalas against Arthas Menethil's Scourge invasion, but was ultimately defeated and killed. Rather than granting her a quick death, Arthas ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee—a vengeful agent of the Lich King powered by hatred.

When the Lich King's control weakened, Sylvanas broke free from his tyranny and reclaimed her body. She gathered other renegade undead and founded the Forsaken, becoming their queen and vowing to avenge her own death. The Forsaken eventually joined the Horde and played a crucial role in defeating the Lich King in Northrend. Sylvanas served as Warchief of the Horde, seeking a higher purpose for her people who had already died once.

However, her reign became increasingly controversial. An uprising within her ranks that killed other Horde members left her mistrusted by many allies. She ultimately became a boss encounter in the Sanctum of Domination, where she fought to protect the Jailer's plans. Stripped of her masks and illusions, the Banshee Queen unleashed her full power in a desperate final stand against Azeroth's champions.

no_adjectives.question: What is the Lich King?
no_adjectives.reference: The Lich King is the ruler of the undead Scourge and resides on the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel in Northrend. The original Lich King was Ner'zhul, an orc shaman whose spirit was trapped in armor by Kil'jaeden. Arthas Menethil later merged with Ner'zhul to become the new Lich King. After Arthas was defeated, Bolvar Fordragon became the new Lich King to keep the Scourge in check.
snippets: Lich King: While the Lich King dreamt, the various personas in his mind - the death knight Arthas Menethil, the orc shaman Ner'zhul, and Matthias Lehner, the personification of the remnants of Arthas's humanity - fought for influence and control over the entity. Matthias tried to reason with Arthas, but Arthas silenced him by running him through with Frostmourne. Ner'zhul was delighted by this, declaring that he and Arthas were now free to merge into a single glorious being. Arthas rejected this offer, stating that once he had the power of the Lich King, no one would tell him what to do again. He impaled the stunned Ner'zhul with Frostmourne, becoming the dominant personality of the Lich King and ending the dream.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, Epilogue In his quiet meditation, the part of the Lich King that was Arthas began to reflect on the events of his life, on the path that had taken him so far from the young boy who who had been his father's pride and joy. In the midst of his reflections, Arthas sensed a bitter and ancient presence locked within the nearby ice, and with this realization came the name Sindragosa.The Cinematic Art of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King Wrath of the Lich King Awakening thumb|upright=1.2|The awakened Lich King and his undead armies in Icecrown.

Lich King (tactics): The Lich King is the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and the final major encounter of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King. This boss has been available since February 2, 2010 on US servers and February 3 on EU servers. The Lich King is a long and challenging battle for survival. All classes are tested to the limits of their abilities, in particular all raid members have to react quickly to various abilities, often several at the same time. The encounter is rather unforgiving; most players must react properly in various situations, and all players get ample opportunity to commit mistakes which then wipe the raid. This encounter is a fitting end to Wrath of the Lich King and on par with Yogg-Saron in Ulduar. Abilities The Lich King melees for roughly 40,000 on an item level 264 geared tank on 25-player difficulty. The Lich King enrages after 15 minutes of combat, gaining 900% damage and 150% attack speed. However, he is notably still affected by taunt abilities. Phase 1 Phase 1.5 and 2.5 Phase 2 The Lich King retains Infest. Phase 3 The Lich King retains Soul Reaper and Defile. Frostmourne Spirit Warden Terenas Menethil Phase 4 Terenas Menethil Preparation The fight requires the standard raid composition of 2/3 Tanks and 3/5 healers (10/25 player), while learning the encounter more healers can help. No resistance or other special gear is required.

Icon of the Lich King: thumb|250px|Icon of the Lich King. The Icon of the Lich King is the sigil of the Scourge. Its azure coloration and icy ornamentation symbolize the frigid homeland of the undead horde in Northrend. At the icon's center is the now-shattered runeblade Frostmourne, the former blade of the Lich King Arthas Menethil. The frost-covered hammers flanking the blade represent the Scourge's endless capacity for warfare, as well as their construction of the saronite citadel Icecrown and the dread Necropoli that shepherded the endless armies of the dead and served as hubs of unspeakable torment upon the living. Although the holder of the Helm of Domination has changed since the creation of the sigil, and the goals of the Scourge along with them, this sigil seems to have remained representative of the organization. The Cult of the Damned, an affiliate group consisting primarily of the living, retains both a unique icon and the twisted ambitions of the original Scourge. Gallery See also Icon Category:Icons

The Birth of the Lich King: "The Birth of the Lich King" is a section of the History of Warcraft, Chapter IV. It is set immediately following the events of Warcraft II: Beyond the Dark Portal. Found at: Reliquary of Scholomance in the Western Plaguelands Southshore Inn in the Hillsbrad Foothills The Scarlet Bastion (live side) of Stratholme The Darkshire town hall in Duskwood, next to Lord Ello Ebonlocke Timeline: ← Previous book: The Invasion of Draenor → Next book: Icecrown and the Frozen Throne Further reading Ner'zhul's escape is detailed in the novel Beyond the Dark Portal. In the comic, when the Scourge attacked Stormwind Harbor and Orgrimmar, a large, shade-like figure speaks similar lines.The Winds of War Quotes Wrath of the Lich King Random Stormwind Harbor Ring of Valor, interrupting Thrall and Garrosh's mak'gora Invasion ends Comic Alliance "Hear me, foolish children of the Alliance! Today, we will forge a new alliance--for today, you will fight and die! And your bodies will rise to join the Lich King's undead army! All hail the Lich King...soon to be king of Stormwind...and all of Azeroth! Horde "Hear me, Orcs of Orgrimmar! Brash upstarts of the Horde! Tremble and know your doom, for the Lich King's gaze is fixed upon you!" Notes During the first Scourge Invasion, the Mouth of Kel'Thuzad yelled the lines. # The Lich King

The Lich King is the supreme ruler of the Scourge, an undead army that serves as one of World of Warcraft's major antagonistic forces. The entity itself is a fusion of multiple consciousnesses: the death knight Arthas Menethil (who became the dominant personality), the orc shaman Ner'zhul, and Matthias Lehner (representing remnants of Arthas's humanity). Arthas eliminated the other personalities during an internal struggle within the Lich King's mind, solidifying his control over the entity and ensuring that no one would ever command him again.

The Lich King wields immense power, including command over countless undead servants and the legendary runeblade Frostmourne. He rules from Icecrown Citadel in Northrend, where he served as the final boss of the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The Lich King is represented by the Icon of the Lich King—the Scourge's sigil featuring Frostmourne at its center, flanked by frost-covered hammers symbolizing the endless capacity for warfare and construction of the saronite citadel and Necropoli that serve as hubs of the Scourge's power.

no_adjectives.question: What is Illidan Stormrage known for?
no_adjectives.reference: Illidan Stormrage is a night elf demon hunter and the twin brother of Malfurion Stormrage. He is known for drinking from the Skull of Gul'dan which transformed him into a demon-like being. He was imprisoned for ten thousand years for creating a new Well of Eternity. After being freed he took control of Outland and the Black Temple. He is often associated with the quote 'You are not prepared.' He was later revealed to be a crucial weapon against the Burning Legion.
snippets: Illidan Stormrage: While Malfurion had spent a lifetime fighting for the dream of what Azeroth could be, he now must fight for what it is. With his final words, Illidan asked Malfurion to take care of Tyrande and listen to her counsel, for she was always the best of them. The road ahead would be long, but whatever came, he knew Malfurion would bring honor to the name Stormrage. To Tyrande, he spoke of how all the paths he had taken had led to a single purpose, saving the world; he could bide no half measures or compromises. He told her how whenever he faced doubt, he held on to one anchor: Her. He told Tyrande that she embodied the best of Azeroth with her faith and devotion, and asked her to take care of Malfurion. While he sometimes wished her heart had made a different choice, in the end he knew she made the right one. Atop Hyjal, on the shores of the Well of Eternity that he created ages ago, Illidan told the adventurer that they had proven their commitment to Azeroth, that their dedication and sacrifice rivaled his own.

The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage: The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage is the continuation of the Illidan Stormrage campaign in Patch 7.1.0. Objectives Seek out Allari the Souleater at Illidari Stand in Azsuna. Description Allari the Souleater, an oracle of the Illidari, has been on the hunt for Illidan's soul for quite some time. She now believes that she has discovered its location and requires your assistance in its recovery. Find Allari at Illidari Stand in Azsuna and aid her in any way possible. If Sargeras truly plans to use Illidan's body as a vessel for his own return then returning Illidan's soul to his body is integral in preventing the dark titan from returning to our world! Rewards You will receive: Completion The naaru heard my call? Interesting... Very well, let's get to work. We've found Illidan's soul - finally - and it's not where we expected it to be held. Progression Patch changes External links

Illidan Stormrage (tactics): Illidan Stormrage is the final boss of the Black Temple. The fight consists of five phases, and good positioning is the key to success. Biography Adventure Guide Overview Illidan wields his Warglaives at the start of the encounter, infecting foes with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Upon reaching 65% health, Illidan takes flight and throws the Warglaives of Azzinoth to the ground, each of which unleashes a Flame. The two Flames of Azzinoth must be defeated to bring Illidan to the ground. For the remainder of the encounter, Illidan periodically unleashes the power of the demonic force within him, using those dark powers to assault his foes, before returning to his natural form. At 30% health, Maiev arrives to assist in handling the Betrayer's final efforts to overpower his foes. file:Dps_icon.png Damage Dealers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Healer_icon.png Healers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Tank_icon.png Tanks Use Active Mitigation defensive abilities to survive Shear. Move Illidan Stormrage to avoid damage taken from Flame Crash. In Stage Two, be quick to grab the attention of the Flames of Azzinoth.

Lord Illidan Stormrage (quest): Lord Illidan Stormrage is the second of the three storyline quests, available starting at reputation with Netherwing. Objectives Meet Illidan Stormrage. Seek out Barthamus in the Lower City of Shattrath should you survive. You will need: Meeting with Illidan Stormrage Description Come, highlord . Illidan himself awaits us! Completion I was the first, you know. The abandoned child of a monster... We owe you everything, . You have single-handedly shattered the Dragonmaw empire and gathered enough information to keep my brethren occupied for ten lifetimes. It is not much but there is something that we can offer... My kin have each offered to join you on your mission in Outland. Simply ask and they will bond with you. Quest Dialogue During this quest, Overlord Mor'ghor summons an image of Illidan at the Dragonmaw Base Camp to meet the "Dragonmaw orc" (the player in disguise) who will be their champion: An image of Illidan appears. Illidan immobilizes you. Illidan's image vanishes. Yarzill the Merc transforms into a nether dragon! Notes thumb|Riding off into the sunset on Yarzill's back At the end of the dialogue sequence, Yarzill transforms into a nether dragon and carries the player to Shattrath City. The trip is slow, as Yarzill flies at about walking speed. It is advised to have the necessary time before triggering this quest, although as of patch 2.3.0 the speed has been raised.

Awakenings: Objectives Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage in Val'sharah. Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage Description Light's Heart is to serve as the vessel for Illidan Stormrage's rebirth: my last act of service to the Light. But Illidan is dead and his soul is lost in the Nether. Before we may attempt to recover his soul, we must prepare the vessel for his return. Across two worlds exist echoes of Illidan's past. You must venture out and find the echoes. Start where all life begins, : birth. Find Illidan's birthplace in Val'sharah. Awaken the memory. Rewards You will receive: Progress When our work is complete, I will be lost to the Great Dark. Completion An auspicious start to a life of tragedy. Perhaps one of the few joyous memories Illidan would have in his life. While the years that followed tested the prophesied child, they would never break him. You must remember that, despite what you may see as our journey continues. There is much more to be done, but I am not yet ready. I will call for you when it is time. Gains 20,560 XP Notes Go to Lorlathil in Val'sharah. This will prompt a message from Xe'ra. Press the extra action button to see the cinematic. thumb|Elves in front of the newborn Stormrage twins. A crowd of night elves has assembled in Lorlathil around two cribs, presided by a lead Priestess of the Moon wearing the Raiment of Divine Clarity set, and six more priestesses each wearing a Mooncloth Robe.
no_adjectives.response:
# Illidan Stormrage

Illidan Stormrage is known as one of World of Warcraft's most complex and tragic figures. He is primarily remembered as **the Betrayer** — a demon hunter who became corrupted by demonic power and went to extreme lengths in his obsession to protect Azeroth from threats, particularly the Burning Legion.

His most defining characteristic is his unwavering, uncompromising dedication to saving the world. Illidan believed that no half measures or compromises were acceptable when fighting for Azeroth's survival. This singular focus drove many of his controversial decisions and his eventual corruption, as he absorbed demonic power to gain the strength he believed was necessary to defend the planet.

Illidan is also known for his complicated personal relationships, particularly his lifelong love for Tyrande Whisperwind, who chose his brother Malfurion instead. Despite this heartbreak, Illidan acknowledged that Tyrande made the right choice and held her as an anchor throughout his struggles.

As a gameplay figure, Illidan served as the final boss of the Black Temple raid, one of World of Warcraft's most iconic encounters, and was central to the Legion expansion's storyline, where players were tasked with recovering his soul to prevent Sargeras from using his body as a vessel for his own return.

snippets: Mystic (Warcraft III): Mystics were night elf units in Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos. They appear only in the night elf mission Brothers in Blood. They have the appearance of human Apprentice Wizards, a variant of the high elf Priests, and are tinted red. They notably cast shamanistic spells and are the only non-druid male night elves in the game. Deals 400 damage to summoned units. Duration (Hero) 15 (5) sec. Bloodlust While Mystics do technically have the Bloodlust ability, it is the Shaman's version, not the neutral version. They do not have it researched, so they can't actually use it. Notes In Reforged, they look like high elves. Speculation The Mystics share a number of similarities with night elven lorekeepers; as with those of the Shen'dralar they are hidden away beneath the earth in places of knowledge, and like the Talrendis Lorekeepers they are also spellcasters. # Mystics in Warcraft III

Mystics are night elf units that appear exclusively in the Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos mission "Brothers in Blood." They were part of the Barrow Deeps Watchers and served as guardians of magical artifacts in the prison's library. Visually, they resemble human Apprentice Wizards with a red tint and were notable as the only non-druid male night elves in the game.

In terms of combat abilities, Mystics cast shamanistic spells. Their primary ability is **Purge**, which removes all buffs from a target unit and slows its movement speed by 80% for 15 seconds, with the unit gradually recovering its speed over time. The spell also deals 400 damage to summoned units. While Mystics technically have access to the Shaman version of Bloodlust, it is not researched and therefore unavailable during gameplay.

In Warcraft III: Reforged, the Mystics' appearance was updated to resemble high elves instead of their original design.

Banshee (Warcraft III): Banshees were once beautiful female Night Elves who were brutally murdered by demons during the fall of Kalimdor. Their restless spirits were left to wander the world for many ages in silent, tortured lamentation. Over the years they became jealous of the living and seeded a deep hatred for all life. Yet when the Lich King, Ner'zhul, became aware of them, he gathered them together and promised them vengeance upon the living. Ner'zhul gave them terrible voices so that the living would finally hear their timeless anguish. Thus, the Banshees have pledged themselves to the Undead cause and serve as valuable warriors in the Scourge. The spectral Banshees can fly and are fond of hammering their enemies with tortured blasts of sonic force. Their unnerving, unholy screams can be heard from miles away. Information Banshees are very good at supporting units, coming with their initial spell of Curse and can later learn Anti-Magic Shell and Possession. They're still fairly weak like Necromancers and need protection. Banshees are the bane of any army that is heavily invested into ground units, as they can steal them and turn them against their owner if given the chance. # Sylvanas Windrunner (Banshee Form) in Warcraft III

## How to Obtain Her

In the original Warcraft III, you get Sylvanas in her banshee form by completing an optional quest during "The Fall of Silvermoon" campaign mission. Upon completion, you receive her as a named banshee unit and unlock the ability to build fully upgraded Banshees.

In Warcraft III: Reforged, the process is simplified—you only need to reach the gates of Silvermoon to defeat her, and you no longer need to complete the optional mission.

## Abilities

Sylvanas in banshee form has access to two primary spells:

- **Curse (Autocast)**: Curses a target enemy unit, causing it to have a 33% chance to miss on attacks.
- **Anti-Magic Shell**: Creates a barrier that grants a target unit spell immunity for 90 seconds, costing 50 mana.

In Reforged, her banshee form retains these core banshee abilities, though some spell modifications were made—notably replacing the Black Arrow spell with Howl of Terror alongside adjustments to the missile effects.

# The Original Harvester of Wrath

The original Harvester of Wrath was one of the venthyr created directly by Sire Denathrius. Long ago, her power was stripped from her and transferred to the Stonewright, who has held the title of Harvester of Wrath ever since. According to the Curator, the first Harvester still exists somewhere, but she hasn't been seen for "a few epochs" and her current whereabouts remain unknown.

The Stonewright now serves as the second Harvester of Wrath and is the mother of the stoneborn. She is formidable enough that the Curator warns against confusing her sympathy for her creations as weakness—she holds the Medallion of Wrath for good reason. During the Chains of Domination campaign, the Stonewright offers her medallion to the Maw Walker to empower the other Harvesters and prevent Denathrius from regaining control of Revendreth.

Lei Shen (tactics): Lei Shen is the twelfth and final boss encounter in the Throne of Thunder on Looking for Raid and Normal Mode. Background Adventure Guide Tyrant. Dictator. Conqueror. Emperor. These titles of the Thunder King were earned by bringing absolute misery to every race of Pandaria. Resurrected into a world where his people no longer rule, Lei Shen is bent on ending the farce and bringing the land under his heel once more. Encounter design The core concept for the Lei Shen fight developed during a brainstorming session as members of the encounter design team were throwing around ideas for potential fight mechanics, trying to see if anything would stick. A popular idea was for him to need to pause and recharge periodically, but someone suggested an interesting twist on that idea—the Thunder King isn't just a battery that needs to recharge; he's actually a power source himself. So instead of him drawing power from his citadel, the citadel draws power from him. He is a mighty warrior in his own right, with distinctive weapon and lightning attacks, but his mere presence energizes and activates portions of his citadel’s defenses, and managing those becomes a central element of the encounter.

# Lei Shen: The Thunder King

Lei Shen was a mogu warlord who became one of World of Warcraft's most significant antagonists during the Mists of Pandaria expansion. He rose from exile to become the Thunder King, dominating Pandaria through conquest and tyranny before being defeated by players in the Throne of Thunder raid.

## His Rise to Power

Lei Shen's path began in tragedy. After his father was betrayed and murdered by a close advisor, his clan fell apart. Rather than continue the destructive mogu infighting, the young mogu entered self-imposed exile, meditating and wandering the land. Most other mogu dismissed his meditations as madness and believed he would never threaten them again—a grave miscalculation. During his exile, Lei Shen studied the legends of his forefathers and developed a vision far greater than simple conquest. Upon reaching adulthood and commanding his own legions, he announced his intent to awaken the gods themselves.

## His Conquest

Lei Shen's search led him to hidden vaults beneath the lands north of the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, where he discovered the titanic watcher Ra-den and the Engine of Nalak'sha—a powerful titan device. Using this knowledge and his own formidable abilities in warfare and the arcane arts, Lei Shen conquered Pandaria and established himself as an absolute ruler, earning the titles of Tyrant, Dictator, Conqueror, and Emperor. When he was eventually resurrected in World of Warcraft's present day, he sought to restore mogu dominion over the land. Players defeated him as the final boss of the Throne of Thunder raid.

# Yol'Tithian

Yol'Tithian was a Highborne sorcerer who played a significant role during the War of the Ancients. He is best known for assisting Dath'Remar Sunstrider in the escape from Zin-Azshari, the capital city of the night elves during this ancient conflict.

As a mage and member of the Highborne elite, Yol'Tithian was among the most powerful sorcerers of his time. His participation in helping Dath'Remar escape Zin-Azshari was crucial, as it allowed a faction of the Highborne to survive the cataclysmic events of the Sundering and eventually establish themselves in the lands that would become the Eastern Kingdoms.

Tushui teachings encourage living a venerable life through meditation, rigorous training, and moral conviction. When the Wandering Isle was opened to the rest of the world, pandaren who upheld the tenets of Tushui were drawn to the Alliance due to their similar philosophies. Since then, they have taken part in many conflicts and events throughout Azeroth alongside their new allies. The Tushui pandaren are contemplative, focused on abstract ideals of justice and morality, and believe that a principled life is the only one worth living. Their idealogical opposites are the Huojin Pandaren, also of the Wandering Isle, but who chose to join with the Horde. Both groups represent the majority of pandaren within their respective factions. # The Wanderer's Way Philosophy

The Wanderer's Way (also called the Traveler's Path) is a pandaren philosophy originating from the wanderlust-stricken inhabitants of the Wandering Isle. It emphasizes taking each journey one step at a time, observing everything around you, speaking to everyone you meet, and absorbing all the details of your surroundings. However, the philosophy also teaches the crucial balance of knowing when to explore and when to stand your ground and fight.

The philosophy is exemplified by Liu Lang, the first pandaren explorer who ventured beyond Pandaria on the back of the giant turtle Shen-zin Su. His spirit of exploration inspired the Wanderer's Festival, where adventurous pandaren gather to celebrate his legacy and their own wanderlust. The philosophy is practiced by known characters including Liu Lang himself, Chen Stormstout, and Li Li Stormstout. Many other pandaren in the open world likely followed this philosophy as well, with Gravewalker Gie and Mojo Stormstout being possible practitioners.

no_adjectives.question: What exactly is a cartel in World of Warcraft, and how are goblins organized into them?
no_adjectives.reference: A cartel is a large group of traders in goblin and Broker society. Goblins are organized into cartels, which are the most powerful groups among goblin society. After goblins ended their conflicts with each other, they formed cartels as rival organizations that compete in trade and economics rather than warfare. These cartels are typically led by trade princes, though not exclusively.

Cartel: A cartel refers to a large group of traders in goblin and Broker society. Goblins After the goblins overthrew the Zandalari trolls, they celebrated their new found freedom by turning on each other, creating chaos as countless feuds were waged and alliances formed. Soon enough they were split into various different groups, with the most powerful of these known as the cartels. However, as the battles raged on and it became clear no one was actually winning, the cartels realized it was in their mutual interests to broker a truce. Despite this, the conflict would never really end, as the goblins now turned to trade, doing battle in the economic arena. For years to come the cartels were rivals, constantly outshining and outsmarting each other as they continued in their quest for wealth and power.World of Warcraft: Chronicle Volume 1, pg. 158 Goblin cartels often compete with other cartels, such as in the Trade Wars. These organizations are normally led by trade princes, but they don't have to be; for example, although the Venture Company is a cartel, its leader Mogul Razdunk is not a trade prince.Mogul Razdunk (tactics) Known cartels Bilgewater Cartel Steamwheedle Cartel Venture CompanyMorin Cloudstalker (quest) Unnamed neutral cartel led by Trade Prince DonaisAsk CDev Answers - Round 4 Brokers The Brokers of the Shadowlands are organized into various cartels.

Goblin: The goblins, having incidentally caused the destruction of their Town-In-A-Box with the volcanic eruption, return to Thrall and the orcs just in time to help stave off the Alliance assault. Impressed with the goblins cunning and strength the orcs agreed to take the fight to Trade Prince Gallywix, which had enslaved the majority of the members of the Bilgewater Cartel and put them to work mining resources. After disrupting Gallywix's operations, the free goblins overthrow Gallywix himself with the assistance of Thrall. Surrendering himself, Gallywix promises the Bilgewater Cartel will reform and become a part of the Horde. Thrall accepts, offering to send a representative to the Horde's new Warchief Garrosh Hellscream and promises them a new home in Azshara and a place within the Horde. The goblins of the Bilgewater Cartel now reside chiefly in Azshara, which they have massively altered. Goblins have also been seen assisting their allies in other areas such as Northern Barrens, Stonetalon Mountains, and Felwood. The goblins have made a home in Orgrimmar where Boss Mida runs the Goblin Slums an area of filth, pollution, and technology, just how the goblins like it.

Bilgewater Cartel: thumb|Banner of the cartel. The Bilgewater Cartel is one of the largest goblin organizations found on Azeroth. Run by the ruthless Trade Prince Gallywix, it is the most powerful trading company on Kezan. From their headquarters at Bilgewater Port, the cartel dominated the trade all over the South Seas,In-game reputation description until it was destroyed due to the Cataclysm. After the events in the Lost Isles, the Bilgewater joined the Horde and built a new capital in the Kalimdorian region of Azshara, Bilgewater Harbor. They compete with other goblin organizations such as the Steamwheedle Cartel and Venture Company over both resources and customers. In the game, the Bilgewater Cartel is a Horde-aligned faction related to the playable Bilgewater goblins, introduced in Cataclysm. It is thus the sixth racial Horde-faction to be added to the game. They have their own quartermaster, rewards, and tabard which in dungeons will boost reputation for every kill. History Originally the slaves of Zandalari trolls on the Isle of Kezan, the goblin race was forced to mine kaja'mite ore out of the volcanic bowels of Mount Kajaro. The trolls used this potent mineral for their voodoo rituals, but it had an unexpected effect on the slaves who were in constant contact with it: kaja'mite generated a startling new cunning and intelligence in the goblins.

Steamwheedle Cartel: The Steamwheedle Cartel is one of the largest and most successful of the goblin cartels in the Undermine. It is a neutral organization that controls several cities and towns around the world, and has allied themselves with a number of other groups such as the Blackwater Raiders in Booty Bay. The Steamwheedle Cartel has made a great deal of profit from maintaining neutrality in the conflict between the Horde and the Alliance. Their services are always available to the highest bidder, employing anyone regardless of their affiliation if they can produce results. Background Great Wars Prior to the First War,:File:Chronicle2 Eastern Kingdoms Before the First War.jpg the goblins of the Steamwheedle Cartel managed to drive out the trolls from a small port town inhabited by humans which had been overrun. They claimed control of the immediate area and the shores of the cove for their new center for operations in the Eastern Kingdoms — Booty Bay.Wharfmaster Dizzywig (Barrens)The Barrens Port: Most likely Velinde found a trading vessel in Ratchet to take her to Blackwater Cove in Azeroth.Passage to Booty Bay: Velinde. Booked passage to Booty Bay on the Black Osprey. During the war, the goblins witnessed the Old Horde's arrival and the conquest of Stormwind. With more conflicts on the horizon, they were determined to profit from it.
no_adjectives.response:
# Goblin Cartels in World of Warcraft

A cartel is a large group of traders in goblin society that emerged after the goblins overthrew their Zandalari troll masters. When the goblins gained freedom, they initially turned on each other in countless feuds and battles. However, as no single faction achieved dominance, the most powerful groups—the cartels—realized mutual cooperation was more profitable than endless conflict. They brokered a truce and shifted their competition from warfare to commerce, battling in the economic arena instead.

Cartels are typically led by trade princes, though this isn't a strict requirement—for example, Mogul Razdunk leads the Venture Company cartel despite not holding a trade prince title. These organizations compete with each other constantly, engaging in what's known as Trade Wars as they vie for wealth, power, and control of trade routes across Azeroth.

The major known cartels include the **Bilgewater Cartel** (now aligned with the Horde), the **Steamwheedle Cartel** (neutral and widespread across the world), and the **Venture Company**. The Steamwheedle Cartel in particular has prospered through strict neutrality, making their services available to the highest bidder regardless of Horde or Alliance affiliation. Goblins also organize into numerous smaller enterprises and organizations beyond the cartels, ranging from engineering corporations to trade fleets and specialized companies.

no_adjectives.question: Who is Bolten Vanyst and what's his connection to Tyrathan Khort?
no_adjectives.reference: Bolten Vanyst is the lord of the Vanyst family to whom Tyrathan Khort had sworn his service. Bolten directed Khort to lead a squad of scouts to the Serpent's Heart in Pandaria, but the mission resulted in an attack by the Sha that killed all scouts except Khort. Feeling guilt over the incident, Khort later believed Bolten used his nephew Morelan as a tool to further his own ambitions during the Pandaria campaign.
snippets: Bolten Vanyst: Bolten Vanyst is is the lord of the Vanyst family of whom Tyrathan Khort had sworn his service too. A vain man with a scheming harridan of a wife, Bolten is always for a great campaign as it takes him away from Stormwind. The father of three daughters, Bolten has seemingly promised his realm to one of their husbands should they be pleased to take up the mantle. Yet when Bolten leaves on a campaign, his nephew- Morelan Vanyst- is always left as regent. When the hidden continent of Pandaria was discovered, Bolten assisted the Alliance in the region. He directed Tyrathan Khort to lead a squad of scouts to the Serpent's Heart and expected them to report within three hours. However the scout force was attacked by the Sha and all but Khort were killed in the ensuing struggle. Driven by guilt, Khort limped away from the battlefield and was eventually taken to the Shado-Pan Monastery to heal by Chen Stormstout. Feeling the need to prove himself after Tyrathan Khort seemingly passed away, Morelan Vanyst journeyed to join the Pandaria campaign where Khort surmises that Bolten was quick to use his nephew as a tool to further his own ambitions.Vol'jin: Shadows of the Horde References Category:Human characters Category:Aristocrats Category:Stormwind (kingdom) Category:Lore characters Category:Vol'jin: Shadows of the Horde characters es:Bolten Vanyst

Tyrathan Khort: Tyrathan Khort is a human hunter in the Alliance army, but not especially high ranking. He was found by Chen Stormstout after being injured in battle and brought to the Shado-Pan Monastery to heal, where the Shado-Pan had him care for the recovering Vol'jin so their opposite personalities would achieve balance. Tyrathan was haunted by doubt after losing his scouting squad to a sha attack in the Jade Forest. He and Vol'jin eventually become friends, even making a pact to kill whomever killed the other. Biography As a boy, Tyrathan was inspired to become a hunter by the legends of the Unseen Path.Tyrathan's gossip text The Khort family has long served the Vanyst family of Stormwind nobles, a tradition which Tyrathan continued. Tyrathan met his wife when he was hunting a pack of wolves, but as time went on his wife grew to worry about how easily killing came to Tyrathan. He feels that while he was away as a soldier, that perhaps his wife and Morelan Vanyst have grown close. Tyrathan served in Daelin Proudmoore's forces that occupied Theramore shortly after the Third War and during that time he met the troll leader, Vol'jin. At some point in the past he spent time in Stranglethorn Vale and learned how to speak Zandali from his troll guide Keren'dal. He speaks Zandali with a Stranglethorn accent. Pandaria Tyrathan's superior, Bolten Vanyst, had him lead a squad of scouts to Serpent's Heart and report back within three hours.

Morelan Vanyst: Morelan Vanyst, the nephew of Bolten Vanyst, is left as the regent lord of the Vanyst family when his uncle leaves on a campaign. Having lost his wife when she gave birth to their son, Morelan would become close with the wife of Tyrathan Khort as their children played together. Hearing and believing of Khort's passing in Pandaria, Morelan would join the Pandaria campaign in order to prove that he could be brave. His uncle, ever a schemer, was quick to use his nephew to further his ambitions. After Tyrathan Khort's efforts against the Zandalari, Khal'ak ordered Captain Nir'zan to discover his identity by attacking the Alliance and taking human prisoners. Morelan and a group of fellow soldiers were subsequently attacked and only the timely intervention of Khort and his allies rescued him from captivity. Hoping and yet fearing, Morelan demanded to know where Tyrathan Khort was; pointing out he would recognize those arrows anywhere and did not believe Vol'jin when he said that there was no Tyrathan Khort. After Vol'jin claimed to have killed Khort, Morelan paled while mentioning the rumors of Vol'jin's death.

Keren'dal: Keren'dal is a jungle troll who served as a guide to Tyrathan Khort during his time in Stranglethorn. Over time Khort learned Zandali from Keren'dal, though at first it was without the troll realizing it. After Khort's time in Stranglethorn was over, the two parted ways; as far as Khort knows, Keren'dal is still alive.Shadows of the Horde, chapter 9 References Category:Jungle troll characters Category:Lore characters Category:Vol'jin: Shadows of the Horde characters es:Keren'dal

Larsi: Larsi was a young hunter who answered the Alliance's call to journey to the newly discovered continent of Pandaria. Meeting Tyrathan Khort on the boat right there, Larsi thanked Khort and admitted that she thought the Pandaria campaign was going to be an adventure. Always there should Khort need something, Khort and Larsi struck up a friendship, though Tyrathan got the sense that she felt that she owed him something for some unknown reason. Larsi was killed by the sha while scouting the Serpent's Heart at the command of Bolten Vanyst. Khort, as the only survivor of the sha attack, was wracked with guilt over her death.Vol'jin: Shadows of the Horde References Category:Human characters Category:Hunter characters Category:Lore characters Category:Vol'jin: Shadows of the Horde characters Category:Deceased characters es:Larsi
no_adjectives.response:
# Bolten Vanyst and Tyrathan Khort

Bolten Vanyst is a Stormwind nobleman and lord of the Vanyst family. He's characterized as a vain man with ambitious schemes, and he has a history of going on military campaigns partly to escape his scheming wife. The Khort family has long served the Vanyst family as retainers, making Tyrathan Khort his subordinate soldier.

During the discovery of Pandaria, Bolten directed Tyrathan to lead a scouting squad to the Serpent's Heart with orders to report back within three hours. This mission ended in disaster when the Sha attacked the scouts, killing everyone except Tyrathan. The sole survivor was consumed with guilt over losing his entire squad, including the young hunter Larsi.

Bolten's actions reveal his manipulative nature—when his nephew Morelan believed Tyrathan had died and joined the Pandaria campaign to prove himself, Bolten was quick to use Morelan as a tool to further his own ambitions. Tyrathan later suspected that Bolten may have also grown close with Tyrathan's own wife while he was away as a soldier, adding personal tension to their already strained military relationship.

no_adjectives.question: What is twinking in WoW?
no_adjectives.reference: Twinking is the practice of equipping a low-level character with the best possible gear available at their level bracket to dominate in PvP. Twinks typically lock their experience gain to stay in a specific bracket and use rare drops, crafted items, and enchants that are unusually powerful for their level.

WoW Tokens can also be purchased or redeemed from the character select screen, to reactivate a currently inactive account. From the Auction House When you purchase a token from the Auction House, you will receive a letter from the Auction House with the token. Overview video FAQ The Basics How do I buy a WoW Token for real money? WoW Tokens will be available for purchase for real money through the World of Warcraft in-game Shop. You can access the Shop through the row of feature buttons next to your character's bags. I need gold! How do I sell a WoW Token to another player? You'll be able to sell WoW Tokens through a dedicated Token exchange in the Auction House, located in a new Game Time section. WoW Tokens cannot be traded or sold any other way. How much gold will I receive when I sell a WoW Token? The gold value of a Token will be determined dynamically based on supply and demand. When you put a Token up for sale, you'll be quoted the amount of gold you'll receive upon a successful sale.

no_adjectives.response:
# Twinking in World of Warcraft

Twinking is the practice of equipping a low-level character with high-quality gear to gain a significant advantage in PvP combat, particularly in low-level battlegrounds. Twinks wear items that are far superior to what a character at that level would normally obtain through standard gameplay.

Examples of twink gear include items like the Sunscale Helmet (a best-in-slot helm for level 49 warriors), the Sentry Cloak (a rare world drop), and the Staff of the Friar (a low-level staff from Blackfathom Deeps). These pieces are carefully selected to maximize a character's power while staying within a specific level bracket for PvP queues.

The goal of twinking is to dominate battlegrounds and PvP encounters against other players who are using standard leveling gear. This creates a stark power imbalance, as twinks significantly outperform non-twink characters at the same level.

Horde: This led to a temporary unity between the two factions when Varok asked Anduin Wrynn, High King of the Alliance, for aid in assaulting Orgrimmar to end both the reign of Sylvanas and the enmity between the Horde and Alliance. Ultimately, the rebellion caused the Horde to lose its top leadership when Sylvanas killed Varok and abandoned the Horde. With his death and her abandonment, the Fourth War is considered over and the two factions have begun talks of peace, though many surmise it may not last or cannot forgive the atrocities of the past.Warchief of the Horde (quest)The Price of Peace Currently the Horde and Alliance are in an "uneasy armistice". The Horde is one of the two factions that player characters belong to―the Alliance being the other―as determined by a given character's race (with the exception of Pandaren which can be both). Besides race, being a member of the Horde or Alliance affects gameplay through what settlements, NPCs, quests, reputation factions and in some cases items and encounters the player may access. Faction mechanics also prevent Horde and Alliance players from talking with each other or making groups together, and most PvP gameplay involves Horde players fighting against Alliance players. The divide between the Horde and Alliance—in regard to gameplay mechanics, story elements, and sometimes in social aspects like faction pride—acts as the core element of the World of Warcraft experience.
no_adjectives.response:
# Horde vs Alliance in World of Warcraft

The Horde and Alliance are the two playable factions in World of Warcraft, with players choosing one based on their character's race. Each faction has distinct races, settlements, NPCs, quests, and reputation systems that create entirely separate gameplay experiences.

The faction divide fundamentally shapes the WoW experience through gameplay mechanics and story. Horde and Alliance players cannot communicate with each other or form groups together, and PvP gameplay typically pits the two factions against each other. Different quests, items, and encounters are available depending on your faction, meaning you access different content and storylines.

Beyond mechanics, the factions represent opposing ideologies and allegiances in Warcraft's world. Throughout WoW's history, their relationship has fluctuated from open warfare to temporary alliances, particularly during the Fourth War when both factions faced existential threats. The core Warcraft experience revolves around the tension between these two factions—their competition, conflict, and occasional cooperation defines the game's narrative and social structure.

no_adjectives.question: What's the deal with Iron Qon in Throne of Thunder? Does he have different phases or is it just a straight DPS fight?
no_adjectives.reference: Iron Qon is not a straight DPS fight — it is a four-phase encounter where Qon switches between different magical quilen champions, each empowered with vicious elemental attacks. This creates four separate intense fights rolled into one, rather than a simple tank-and-spank. The overall fight involves tank-and-spank mechanics combined with environment awareness mechanics.
snippets: Iron Qon: Iron Qon is the tenth boss encounter in the Throne of Thunder. It is a tank and spank with numerous environment awareness mechanics. Background Adventure Guide Legends speak of the brutality of Qon and his quilen champions. The fearless mogu commander was dubbed "Iron Qon" by the Thunder King for his unrelenting resolve to claim victory after victory for the empire regardless of the cost. Encounter Design Qon is one of Lei Shen's elite personal guard, and a master of mounted combat. An original concept for this fight was inspired by the Three Kingdoms warlord Lu Bu, a fierce warrior renowned for his skill in fighting with a spear from horseback. Though Qon does have some very cool and unique mounted attack animations, in the end we found it more interesting to explore gameplay via the different abilities of his mounts instead. Thus, the encounter involves Qon switching among different magical quilen, each of which is empowered with vicious elemental attacks, creating a four-phase encounter that is almost like four separate intense fights rolled into one.Patch 5.2 Raid Preview: Throne of Thunder Spells and abilities The Quilen Champions - Iron Qon battles atop his finest Quilen champions.

Primordius: Primordius is the eighth boss encounter in the Throne of Thunder. The fight is a relatively simple DPS race compared to the rest of the fights in the Throne of Thunder. Background Adventure Guide Deep within the bowels of Lei Shen's island citadel, mogu flesh-shapers formed the saurok out of a magical substance of unknown origin. When the citadel was abandoned, some experiments languished in the dark pools for far too long... Encounter Design The fact that the mogu created the saurok (among other creatures native to Pandaria) is something that may not have been obvious to players who were exploring the continent and learning about its inhabitants. This encounter provided us with a chance to "show, not tell" this piece of Pandaria's history. The actual mechanics of the fight evolved from discussion and brainstorming around the themes of evolution and mutation; a few of us had also recently watched the movie Prometheus, and the vaults of black goo in that movie helped inspire the overall vibe of Primordius’ lair.Patch 5.2 Raid Preview: Throne of Thunder Spells and abilities Mutation: Ventral Sacs - Sacs located around Primordius's frill will periodically erupt, inflicting 20,000 Nature damage to all players every 1 sec. Mutation: Gas Bladder - A volatile mutation that allows Primordius to spray Caustic Gas, inflicting 4,050,000 Nature damage split evenly between all players within a 18-yard cone.

no_adjectives.response:
# Iron Qon: A Multi-Phase Encounter

Iron Qon is the tenth boss in Throne of Thunder and features a unique four-phase structure rather than a straightforward DPS race. He's one of Lei Shen's elite personal guard and a master of mounted combat who battles atop different magical quilen champions throughout the fight.

Each of Qon's four phases involves him switching to a different quilen mount, with each one empowered by distinct elemental abilities. This design approach—inspired by the concept of the legendary warlord Lu Bu fighting from horseback—creates what amounts to four separate intense encounters rolled into one. Rather than focusing on mounted attack animations, the developers made the gameplay revolve around the unique abilities of each quilen, making environment awareness and adaptability key mechanics.

While the encounter does function as a tank-and-spank at its core, the multiple phase transitions and different elemental mechanics from each quilen make it significantly more dynamic than a pure DPS race. You'll need to manage the specific threats posed by each mount rather than simply dealing consistent damage throughout.

no_adjectives.question: What's the best aspect to use when I'm fighting enemies up close as a hunter?
no_adjectives.reference: According to the article, Aspect of the Monkey was the best Animal Aspect to use while engaged in melee combat prior to attaining Aspect of the Dragonhawk. It was a defensive aspect that increased dodge and lowered your damage taken when fighting enemies up close.
snippets: Aspect of the Monkey: Aspect of the Monkey was a defensive Animal Aspect for increasing dodge; also the first aspect a hunter used to receive from training. Uses This was the best Animal Aspect to use while engaged in melee combat prior to attaining Aspect of the Dragonhawk. Lowered your damage taken. Limitations Shares a 1-second cooldown with other hunter aspects. Warriors can potentially do more damage to you with the aspect on due to Overpower. Upgrades Improved Aspect of the Monkey (Exotic Beasts Talent): Increases the Dodge bonus of your Aspect of the Monkey by 2% per rank (3 ranks total). Patch changes See also Aspect of the Monkey (Fires of Outland), TCG card References External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Aspect of the Chameleon: Aspect of the Chameleon is a hunter ability, learned with the item The Art of Concealment. It is a disruptive PvP ability, removing the hunter from tracking hunter (Track Humanoids) or druid's (Track Humanoid) minimap for 1 minute. Uses This aspect can be used in Warsong Gulch when assaulting; defending hunters and druids would often turn on their tracking so they locate and ping runners or target enemies before enemies appear on screen. Patch changes External links Category:Hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Aspect of the Pack: Aspect of the Pack increases the party and raid's movement speed but makes them vulnerable to daze; a group version of Aspect of the Cheetah. Glyphs Notes This ability allows players to get to places faster and move quicker in battle. A handy speed boost indoors (where mounts do not work) especially after instance wipes. Generally, a hunter should wait until the last member of the group enters an area before activating this Aspect; the stragglers will eventually catch up to those in front, who will then get the buff themselves. By level 20, players have access to mounts which let them travel at +60% speed (though they are not usable in combat while this Aspect is). Your party member(s) will be dazed when hit, which is very annoying and potentially lethal (hence the term "Aspect of the Dazed" or "Aspect of the Wipe"). A hunter must be careful when to toggle off this ability as to not slow party members. There are addons that can automatically remove the aspect when someone is dazed. Aspect of the Pack does not increase speed if swimming, nor does it affect mount speed. Patch changes External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells Category:Auras Category:Snares and slows

Aspect of the Fox: Aspect of the Fox was an Animal Aspect that allowed all members of the hunter's party or raid to use any casted ability while moving. Notes If timed properly, a hunter could use this to mount up while moving. Patch changes External links Category:Removed hunter abilities Category:Nature spells

Animal Aspects: An Animal Aspect is a self-buff that is modeled after different types of animals. The ability to assume the aspect of an animal is exclusive to hunters. Hunters can learn a total of three or four different Aspects, depending on specialization. Varieties Aspect of the Chameleon: The Hunter takes on the aspect of a chameleon, becoming untrackable. Aspect of the Cheetah: Increases the hunter's movement speed by 90% for 3 sec, and then by 30% for another 9 sec. Aspect of the Eagle (Survival): Grants the hunter and their pet 10% increased Critical Strike chance on all abilities, and increases their chance to gain charges of Mongoose Bite by 100%. Aspect of the Turtle: Deflects all attacks and reduces all damage the hunter take by 30% for 8 sec, but they cannot attack. Aspect of the Wild (Beast Mastery): Grants the hunter and their pet 10 Focus per 1 sec and 10% increased critical strike chance on all attacks for 10 sec. Past varieties This aspect was removed in Patch 7.0.3: Aspect of the Pack: The hunter's group (including the hunter) takes on the characteristics of a pack of cheetahs, increasing all their movement speeds by 30%. If a pack member takes damage, that member will be dazed for 4 sec. This aspect was removed in Patch 6.2.0: Aspect of the Fox: The hunter takes on the aspects of a fox, allowing your party and raid members to cast while moving for 6 seconds. Redesigned and re-added in Patch 6.0.2.
no_adjectives.response:
# Best Aspect for Melee Combat

**Aspect of the Turtle** is your best choice for close-quarters combat. It deflects all attacks and reduces all damage you take by 30% for 8 seconds, providing substantial defensive protection when enemies are right on top of you.

If you're looking for offensive capability alongside defense, **Aspect of the Eagle** (Survival spec) grants you 10% increased Critical Strike chance on all abilities, which helps you deal more damage while staying in melee range. However, this doesn't provide the same damage reduction as the Turtle.

Historically, **Aspect of the Monkey** was the primary melee aspect for hunters before it was removed, as it increased dodge to reduce damage taken. It was considered the best option for melee engagement before the Dragonhawk aspect became available, but it's no longer in the game.

A guardian was set to watch over each, lest the raven's enemies attempt to free them. The first of the shrines is the eagle's, found in the hills to the south of Lake Jorune and west of Stonebreaker Hold in Terokkar Forest. The tablet says that the eagle fought valiantly, but was overcome by a swarm of enemies. The armor and endurance of your bear form is well-suited to this fight. The tablet describes the hawk's guardian as nearly invulnerable to the bird's claws. Forsake physical attacks and call down the fury of the stars to defeat this foe. Do not repeat the hawk's mistake. no_adjectives.response:
# The Eagle's Essence Quest Guide

The **Shrine of the Eagle** is located in Terokkar Forest at coordinates **46, 45**, in the hills south of Lake Jorune and west of Stonebreaker Hold. Fly to that location and simply click on the shrine to summon the Guardian of the Eagle.

Once you've defeated the Guardian and obtained the Essence of the Eagle, use **Arthorn's Sparrowhawk Whistle** (which you should have been given at the start of the quest) to call Arthorn's Sparrowhawk and relay news of your success back to Arthorn Windsong at Evergrove.

The fight can be soloed, and the quest description recommends using your bear form for its armor and endurance. If you lose the whistle for any reason, return to Arthorn Windsong at Evergrove for a replacement.

no_adjectives.question: How do I turn my Lightwell into Lightspring as a priest?
no_adjectives.reference: To turn your Lightwell into Lightspring as a priest, you need to use the Glyph of Lightspring. This glyph transforms Lightwell into Lightspring.
snippets: Lightspring: Lightspring is a priest ability granted by Glyph of Lightspring. It transforms Lightwell into Lightspring. Patch changes External links

Lightwell: thumb|Lightwell Lightwell is a Holy priest ability that conjures a very shiny healing well. Every second it will attempt to give party and raid members a 6 second buff (Lightwell Renew) that heals for a notable amount while the buff is active. It is an exclusive choice node with Lightweaver. History thumb|In the TCG. Prior to patch 5.4.0, Lightwell did not heal targets automatically but instead required players to click on the well in order to receive healing. This functionality engendered a significant amount of complaints from priests and non-priests alike, although some argued that the greatest factor in the failure of Lightwell as a useful healing tool lay in the lack of education of the playerbase. Over time Blizzard granted progressive buffs to Lightwell, but the spell failed to receive whole-hearted acceptance by most players due to the requirement for their own active interaction, preferring to leave the healing to the healers. Patch 5.0.4 introduced Glyph of Lightspring, providing priests with an alternative functionality for the well. Apparently this alternative was well-received, as with Patch 5.4.0 the new design was adopted as the default functionality of Lightwell. Trivia One of the loading screen tips once read "Click on the Lightwell... unless it's a Lightspring!". The tip was originally simply "Click on the Lightwell!", but this was changed some time after the introduction of the now defunct Glyph of Lightspring.
Glyph of Lightspring: Glyph of Lightspring is created with Inscription (260). Patch changes External links Category:Removed priest glyphs
Glyph of Lightwell: Source This item is created with Inscription (150); discovered through Research: Jadefire Ink Patch changes External links Category:Removed priest glyphs

Argent Lightwell Charm: thumb|Argent Lightwell Charm The Argent Lightwell Charm is a :lightwell provided as an objective for The Corpsebeasts. Abilities Patch changes External links Argent Lightwell Charm Argent Lightwell Category:Lightwell
no_adjectives.response:
# Turning Lightwell into Lightspring

Lightspring was accessed through the **Glyph of Lightspring**, a priest glyph created with Inscription (skill level 260). This glyph transformed your Lightwell into Lightspring, which automatically healed nearby party and raid members instead of requiring them to click on the well.

However, the Glyph of Lightspring is now **removed** and no longer available in the game. In Patch 5.4.0, Blizzard made Lightspring's automatic healing the default functionality for Lightwell itself, eliminating the need for the glyph. So if you're playing current World of Warcraft, your Lightwell already works like the old Lightspring did—it automatically casts a healing buff on nearby players without requiring clicks.
